canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_1.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining access control policies and procedures to govern the management of system access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_1.remediation: Develop and document formal access control policies and procedures, ensuring they are reviewed and updated regularly. Implement a process for policy dissemination and training to ensure all personnel are aware of their responsibilities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_10.description: This control requires limiting the number of concurrent sessions for each user account to prevent unauthorized access and resource exhaustion.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_10.remediation: Configure session limits in identity and access management systems, such as AWS IAM or Azure AD, to restrict concurrent logins. Regularly audit session logs to enforce compliance and adjust limits based on operational needs.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_11.description: This control requires implementing device lock mechanisms to protect against unauthorized access when devices are unattended.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_11.remediation: Enforce device lock policies, such as screen lock timeouts and password requirements, on all managed devices. Use mobile device management tools to configure and monitor compliance across endpoints.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_12.description: This control requires terminating user sessions automatically after a period of inactivity or upon specific events to reduce security risks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_12.remediation: Set session timeout configurations in applications and systems, ensuring they align with security policies. Implement automated session termination in cloud services and regularly review logs for anomalies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_14.description: This control requires identifying and documenting actions that can be performed without identification or authentication, ensuring they are limited and justified.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_14.remediation: Review system configurations to list permitted anonymous actions, such as public read access to specific resources. Restrict these actions to minimal necessary functions and document justifications in security policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_17.description: This control requires securing remote access to systems, including encryption and access controls, to prevent unauthorized entry.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_17.remediation: Use VPNs or private endpoints for remote access, enforce multi-factor authentication, and encrypt data in transit. Regularly audit remote access configurations and revoke unnecessary permissions using IaC tools like Terraform.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_18.description: This control requires securing wireless network access through encryption, authentication, and monitoring to protect against eavesdropping.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_18.remediation: Implement WPA3 encryption, use strong authentication methods, and segment wireless networks from critical systems. Regularly scan for rogue access points and update wireless security policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_19.description: This control requires applying access controls to mobile devices, including encryption and remote wipe capabilities, to protect sensitive data.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_19.remediation: Deploy mobile device management solutions to enforce encryption, password policies, and remote wipe. Regularly audit device compliance and restrict access to corporate resources based on device health.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_2.description: This control requires managing user accounts, including creation, modification, and deletion, to ensure proper access lifecycle management.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_2.remediation: Automate account provisioning and deprovisioning using IAM tools, enforce least privilege principles, and regularly review unused accounts. Implement RBAC policies in cloud environments to streamline access controls.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_20.description: This control requires governing the use of external systems, ensuring they meet security standards before accessing organizational resources.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_20.remediation: Establish a vetting process for external systems, including security assessments and compliance checks. Use network segmentation and access controls to limit interactions and monitor for unauthorized access.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_21.description: This control requires managing information sharing with external entities through agreements and security measures to protect data integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_21.remediation: Create formal data sharing agreements that outline security requirements and responsibilities. Encrypt shared data and use secure transfer protocols, while auditing sharing activities for compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_22.description: This control requires controlling publicly accessible content to prevent unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_22.remediation: Regularly scan for publicly exposed resources, such as S3 buckets or storage accounts, and restrict access using IAM policies. Implement content review processes before publishing and use encryption for sensitive data.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_3.description: This control requires enforcing access decisions based on authorized permissions to ensure users can only access resources they are permitted to.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_3.remediation: Implement access control lists and IAM policies that explicitly grant or deny permissions. Use role-based access control and regularly audit permissions to ensure compliance with least privilege principles.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_4.description: This control requires enforcing information flow policies to control how data moves between systems and users, preventing unauthorized transfers.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_4.remediation: Use network segmentation, firewalls, and data loss prevention tools to monitor and restrict data flows. Implement encryption for data in transit and define clear policies for information sharing across boundaries.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_5.description: This control requires separating duties among individuals to reduce the risk of fraud or error by ensuring no single person has excessive control.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_5.remediation: Define roles with distinct responsibilities in IAM systems, such as separating administrative and auditing functions. Use policy as code to enforce separation and regularly review role assignments for conflicts.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_6.description: This control requires granting users the minimum permissions necessary to perform their job functions, reducing the attack surface.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_6.remediation: Implement least privilege access by using IAM roles with scoped permissions and avoiding wildcard policies. Regularly audit permissions with automated tools and revoke unnecessary access through RBAC configurations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_7.description: This control requires limiting unsuccessful logon attempts to prevent brute-force attacks and unauthorized access attempts.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_7.remediation: Configure account lockout policies after a defined number of failed attempts in authentication systems. Monitor logon logs for patterns and implement multi-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_8.description: This control requires displaying system use notifications to inform users of monitoring and acceptable use policies before granting access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AC_8.remediation: Implement banner messages at logon points that notify users of monitoring and legal obligations. Ensure notifications are clear and updated regularly, and document the process in security policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_1.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining awareness and training policies and procedures to educate personnel on security responsibilities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_1.remediation: Develop formal training policies, schedule regular security awareness sessions, and document completion records. Update training materials to reflect current threats and ensure all employees participate annually.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_2.description: This control requires providing literacy training and awareness programs to ensure personnel understand security risks and best practices.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_2.remediation: Conduct mandatory security awareness training covering topics like phishing and data handling. Use interactive modules and assessments to measure understanding, and provide refresher courses periodically.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_3.description: This control requires delivering role-based training tailored to specific job functions to address unique security responsibilities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_3.remediation: Identify security training needs for different roles, such as administrators or developers, and provide specialized courses. Track completion through a learning management system and update content based on role changes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_4.description: This control requires maintaining records of security training completion to demonstrate compliance and track personnel proficiency.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AT_4.remediation: Use a centralized system to log training attendance and completion dates. Regularly audit records for gaps and ensure they are retained as per policy, updating them when new training is provided.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_1.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining audit and accountability policies and procedures to govern logging and monitoring activities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_1.remediation: Document audit policies that define what events to log, retention periods, and review processes. Ensure procedures are communicated to relevant teams and integrated into the overall security framework.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_11.description: This control requires retaining audit records for a defined period to support investigations, compliance, and forensic analysis.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_11.remediation: Configure log retention settings in cloud services to meet regulatory requirements, such as using AWS CloudTrail or Azure Monitor. Regularly review retention policies and archive logs securely to prevent tampering.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_12.description: This control requires generating audit records for system events to enable monitoring, detection, and analysis of security incidents.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_12.remediation: Enable logging for all critical systems, such as API gateways and databases, and ensure logs capture relevant details. Use SIEM tools to centralize and analyze logs, and configure alerts for suspicious activities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_2.description: This control requires enabling event logging for all system components to capture security-relevant events, such as API access and user activities, for audit and monitoring purposes.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_2.remediation: Enable logging on all API gateways and services using IaC tools like Terraform or CloudFormation to ensure consistent configuration. Configure RBAC to restrict log access to authorized personnel only, and ensure logs are encrypted in transit and at rest.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_3.description: This control mandates that audit records contain sufficient detail, including timestamps, user identities, and event outcomes, to support forensic analysis and compliance reporting.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_3.remediation: Configure logging settings in cloud services to include required fields like user IDs and event types using IaC. Implement RBAC to control access to audit logs and enable encryption to protect sensitive log data from unauthorized access.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_4.description: This control requires ensuring adequate storage capacity for audit logs to prevent loss of data due to overflow or insufficient resources.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_4.remediation: Establish procedures to monitor log storage usage and set up alerts for capacity thresholds. Regularly review and adjust storage allocations based on log volume and retention policies, and document these processes in organizational guidelines.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_5.description: This control involves defining and implementing responses to failures in audit logging processes, such as log corruption or service outages, to maintain audit integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_5.remediation: Develop incident response procedures that include steps to identify, report, and remediate logging failures. Train staff on these procedures and conduct regular drills to ensure readiness, documenting all actions in compliance records.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_6.description: This control requires regular review, analysis, and reporting of audit records to detect anomalies, security incidents, and compliance violations.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_6.remediation: Set up automated log analysis tools and metric filters in cloud monitoring services using IaC. Configure alerts for suspicious activities and schedule periodic reviews by security teams, ensuring RBAC limits access to audit data.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_7.description: This control focuses on reducing audit record volume through filtering and aggregation while still generating meaningful reports for security oversight.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_7.remediation: Define procedures for configuring log filters and aggregation rules to focus on high-priority events. Train personnel on report generation techniques and regularly update these processes to align with evolving threats and compliance needs.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_8.description: This control ensures that audit records include accurate and synchronized timestamps to support event correlation and timeline analysis across systems.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_8.remediation: Configure time synchronization settings in cloud services using IaC, such as enabling NTP or using managed time services. Implement RBAC to prevent tampering with timestamp settings and encrypt log data to maintain integrity.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_9.description: This control requires protecting audit information from unauthorized access, modification, or deletion through encryption, access controls, and integrity checks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_AU_9.remediation: Enable file validation and encryption for audit logs in cloud services using IaC. Apply RBAC to restrict log access to authorized roles and use policies to prevent deletion, ensuring logs are stored in secure, tamper-evident locations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_1.description: This control involves establishing and maintaining policies and procedures for security assessment and authorization processes within the organization.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_1.remediation: Document assessment policies and procedures in organizational guidelines, and regularly review them for updates. Train staff on these documents and ensure they are accessible to relevant personnel, with periodic audits to verify compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_2.description: This control requires conducting regular security control assessments to evaluate effectiveness and identify gaps in the security posture.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_2.remediation: Enable automated security assessment tools like GuardDuty or Security Center using IaC. Configure RBAC to limit assessment permissions and schedule regular scans, with findings reviewed and remediated through documented action plans.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_3.description: This control governs the secure exchange of information between internal and external systems, ensuring data integrity and confidentiality during transfers.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_3.remediation: Establish procedures for using encrypted channels and authentication mechanisms for data exchanges. Train personnel on secure communication protocols and document exchange agreements, with regular reviews to update based on risk assessments.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_5.description: This control involves creating and maintaining a plan of action and milestones (POA&M) to address security deficiencies and track remediation efforts.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_5.remediation: Develop a POA&M template and procedures for documenting findings, assigning responsibilities, and setting deadlines. Regularly update the plan based on assessment results and report progress to management, ensuring alignment with organizational goals.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_6.description: This control requires formal authorization for information systems to operate, based on risk assessments and compliance with security requirements.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_6.remediation: Implement an authorization process that includes risk assessments and approval from designated officials. Document authorization decisions and review them periodically, with training for staff on roles and responsibilities in the authorization lifecycle.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_7.description: This control mandates continuous monitoring of security controls and system configurations to detect and respond to changes or threats in real-time.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_7.remediation: Set up automated monitoring tools and alerts for configuration changes using IaC. Configure RBAC to restrict monitoring access and regularly review logs and reports, ensuring encryption protects monitoring data from unauthorized access.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_9.description: This control ensures secure internal system connections by enforcing encryption and authentication, such as TLS, to protect data in transit.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CA_9.remediation: Configure internal connections to use TLS 1.2 or higher and client certificates using IaC. Apply RBAC to manage connection settings and enable encryption for all data transfers, with regular audits to verify compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_1.description: This control involves developing and maintaining configuration management policies and procedures to govern system changes and baseline configurations.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_1.remediation: Document configuration management policies in organizational guidelines and ensure they are reviewed regularly. Train staff on these procedures and establish a change control board to oversee compliance, with periodic audits to validate adherence.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_10.description: This control restricts software usage to authorized applications and versions to prevent security risks from unapproved or outdated software.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_10.remediation: Establish procedures for approving and inventorying software, and enforce restrictions through organizational policies. Train users on compliance requirements and conduct regular audits to detect unauthorized software, updating policies as needed.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_11.description: This control limits user-installed software to prevent unauthorized applications that could introduce vulnerabilities or compliance issues.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_11.remediation: Implement policies and technical controls, such as RBAC or endpoint management tools, to restrict software installation. Train users on allowed software and monitor for violations, with procedures to remove unauthorized applications promptly.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_2.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining baseline configurations for systems to ensure consistency and security across the environment.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_2.remediation: Use IaC tools like Terraform to define and deploy baseline configurations. Apply RBAC to control changes and enable encryption for configuration data, with regular audits to verify compliance with established baselines.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_3.description: This control involves managing configuration changes through a formal process to prevent unauthorized modifications and maintain system integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_3.remediation: Implement change control procedures and use IaC to enforce deletion protection and other safeguards. Configure RBAC to restrict change permissions and encrypt configuration data, with regular reviews to track and audit changes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_4.description: This control requires conducting impact analyses for proposed configuration changes to assess risks and ensure they align with security objectives.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_4.remediation: Establish procedures for performing impact analyses before approving changes, including risk assessments and stakeholder reviews. Document findings and update policies based on lessons learned, with training for staff on analysis techniques.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_5.description: This control restricts access to configuration changes to authorized personnel only, preventing unauthorized modifications that could compromise security.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_5.remediation: Define RBAC policies to limit change access to specific roles and enforce them through IAM tools. Implement approval workflows and audit logs for changes, with regular reviews to ensure compliance and update access as needed.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_6.description: This control mandates configuring security settings, such as multi-region logging and encryption, to protect system integrity and data confidentiality.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_6.remediation: Use IaC to enable multi-region logging and encryption settings across cloud services. Apply RBAC to manage configurations and regularly audit settings to ensure they meet security standards, with encryption for all sensitive data.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_7.description: This control enforces least functionality by restricting network and system access to only necessary ports and services, reducing attack surface.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_7.remediation: Configure network ACLs and firewalls using IaC to deny unnecessary ingress rules. Apply RBAC to manage access controls and enable encryption for data in transit, with regular scans to detect and remediate overly permissive rules.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_8.description: This control requires maintaining an inventory of system components to track assets, manage configurations, and support security assessments.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_8.remediation: Use automated tools like SSM or IaC to inventory and manage system components. Apply RBAC to control inventory access and encrypt inventory data, with regular audits to ensure accuracy and completeness of the asset list.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_9.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining a configuration management plan to track and control changes to system configurations, ensuring consistency and security across cloud environments.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CM_9.remediation: Implement Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools like Terraform or CloudFormation to manage configurations. Use version control systems to track changes and enforce policies through automated compliance checks in CI/CD pipelines.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_1.description: This control requires developing and documenting policies and procedures for contingency planning to ensure organizational readiness for disruptions.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_1.remediation: Create formal contingency policy documents and conduct regular reviews with stakeholders. Establish procedures for updating policies based on risk assessments and ensure staff training on these processes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_10.description: This control requires implementing processes for system recovery and reconstitution after a disruption to restore operations to a known secure state.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_10.remediation: Develop and test recovery playbooks that include data restoration from backups and system reconfiguration. Use automated tools for failover and ensure regular drills to validate recovery procedures.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_2.description: This control requires creating and maintaining a contingency plan to address potential disruptions, including backup and recovery strategies for cloud resources.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_2.remediation: Enable deletion protection on critical resources like load balancers and databases using cloud-native features. Implement IaC to define contingency configurations and use monitoring to alert on plan deviations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_3.description: This control requires providing contingency training to personnel to ensure they understand roles and procedures during disruptions.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_3.remediation: Schedule regular training sessions and simulations for contingency scenarios. Document training materials and track participation to ensure all relevant staff are prepared and updated on procedures.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_4.description: This control requires testing the contingency plan periodically to validate its effectiveness and identify areas for improvement.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_4.remediation: Conduct tabletop exercises and full-scale drills to test contingency plans. Document test results, update plans based on findings, and ensure stakeholders review and approve changes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_6.description: This control requires establishing an alternate storage site to ensure data availability and integrity in case of primary site failure.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_6.remediation: Configure automated backups and replication to geographically separate storage using cloud services like AWS EFS backups or Azure Backup. Use IaC to enforce backup policies and monitor compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_7.description: This control requires setting up an alternate processing site to maintain operations during outages, ensuring business continuity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_7.remediation: Design multi-region or hybrid cloud architectures with failover capabilities. Implement load balancing and auto-scaling, and document procedures for switching to alternate sites during incidents.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_8.description: This control requires ensuring telecommunications services are resilient and available to support operations during contingencies.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_8.remediation: Use redundant network connections and cloud-based telecommunication services. Implement monitoring for service health and establish SLAs with providers to guarantee uptime and quick recovery.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_9.description: This control requires implementing system backup procedures to protect data and enable recovery in case of loss or corruption.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_CP_9.remediation: Enable point-in-time recovery (PITR) for databases and storage systems using cloud-native features. Schedule regular backups, test restoration processes, and use encryption for backup data security.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_1.description: This control requires developing and documenting policies and procedures for identification and authentication to secure access to systems.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_1.remediation: Create formal IAM policies that define user roles and authentication methods. Regularly review and update procedures based on security audits and ensure staff are trained on compliance requirements.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_2.description: This control requires implementing strong identification and authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), for organizational users.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_2.remediation: Enforce MFA for all user accounts using cloud IAM tools. Use role-based access control (RBAC) to limit permissions and regularly audit access logs to detect unauthorized activities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_3.description: This control requires authenticating devices before granting access to systems to prevent unauthorized device connections.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_3.remediation: Implement device certificates or hardware-based authentication. Use network access control (NAC) policies and monitor device compliance through endpoint management tools.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_4.description: This control requires managing identifiers, such as user accounts and access keys, to prevent misuse and ensure accountability.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_4.remediation: Disable root or owner access keys and use least-privilege principles for IAM roles. Implement automated key rotation and audit trails to track identifier usage and changes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_5.description: This control requires managing authenticators, like passwords, to enforce strong security policies and prevent credential-based attacks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_5.remediation: Set password policies with minimum length and complexity requirements using cloud IAM settings. Encourage use of password managers and implement account lockout mechanisms after failed attempts.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_6.description: This control requires providing feedback during authentication processes to inform users of status without revealing sensitive information.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_6.remediation: Configure authentication systems to display generic error messages. Avoid disclosing specific account details in feedback and log authentication attempts for security monitoring.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_7.description: This control requires using cryptographic modules for authentication to ensure secure and verifiable identity validation.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_7.remediation: Deploy FIPS-validated cryptographic modules in cloud services. Use hardware security modules (HSMs) for key management and ensure encryption protocols meet organizational standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_8.description: This control requires implementing identification and authentication for non-organizational users, such as customers or partners, to secure external access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IA_8.remediation: Use federated identity solutions like SAML or OAuth for external user authentication. Apply access controls and monitor sessions to prevent unauthorized access from non-organizational entities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_1.description: This control requires developing and documenting incident response policies and procedures to handle security events effectively.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_1.remediation: Create an incident response plan with defined roles and escalation paths. Regularly update procedures based on lessons learned and ensure all team members are trained on response protocols.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_2.description: This control requires providing incident response training to personnel to ensure they can effectively respond to and manage security incidents.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_2.remediation: Conduct regular training sessions and simulations for incident scenarios. Document training outcomes and update materials to reflect new threats and response techniques.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_3.description: This control requires testing incident response capabilities periodically to validate procedures and improve readiness.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_3.remediation: Perform tabletop exercises and red team drills to test response plans. Analyze results to identify gaps and update procedures, ensuring stakeholders review and approve changes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_4.description: This control requires implementing incident handling processes to detect, analyze, and respond to security incidents promptly.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_4.remediation: Use security tools like GuardDuty or Security Center to monitor for high-severity findings. Automate alerting and response workflows, and conduct root cause analysis to prevent recurrence.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_5.description: This control requires monitoring systems for incidents to enable early detection and continuous oversight of security events.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_5.remediation: Implement centralized logging and SIEM solutions to aggregate and analyze security logs. Set up real-time alerts and dashboards to track incident metrics and trends.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_6.description: This control requires establishing procedures for incident reporting to ensure timely communication and compliance with regulatory requirements.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_6.remediation: Define reporting channels and templates for incident documentation. Train staff on reporting protocols and integrate with external agencies as needed for compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_7.description: This control requires providing incident response assistance, such as support from external experts, to enhance response capabilities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_7.remediation: Establish partnerships with third-party incident response teams. Document contact procedures and conduct joint exercises to ensure effective collaboration during incidents.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_8.description: This control requires the development, documentation, and implementation of an incident response plan to address security incidents affecting cloud systems and data.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_IR_8.remediation: Establish a formal incident response plan that includes roles, responsibilities, communication protocols, and procedures for detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery. Regularly review and update the plan through tabletop exercises and ensure all personnel are trained on their roles.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_1.description: This control requires the establishment of policies and procedures for managing and performing maintenance activities on cloud systems and components.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_1.remediation: Develop and document maintenance policies that outline schedules, approval processes, and security measures for system updates and repairs. Implement procedures to track maintenance activities and ensure compliance with organizational standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_2.description: This control requires that maintenance activities on cloud systems are performed in a controlled manner with proper authorization and oversight.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_2.remediation: Implement change management processes to authorize and log all maintenance actions. Use role-based access controls to restrict maintenance privileges and ensure activities are monitored and audited for compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_3.description: This control requires that maintenance tools used on cloud systems are inspected and controlled to prevent malicious use or unauthorized access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_3.remediation: Maintain an inventory of approved maintenance tools and regularly scan them for malware or vulnerabilities. Restrict tool usage to authorized personnel and implement logging to track tool access and activities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_4.description: This control requires that nonlocal maintenance sessions for cloud systems are secured with strong authentication and encryption to prevent unauthorized access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_4.remediation: Enforce strong password policies with a minimum length of 14 characters and enable multi-factor authentication for remote maintenance access. Use encrypted connections like SSH or VPNs and monitor sessions for suspicious activity.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_5.description: This control requires that personnel performing maintenance on cloud systems are properly trained, authorized, and supervised.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_5.remediation: Implement vetting and training programs for maintenance personnel, and assign roles with least privilege access. Conduct regular audits of maintenance activities and ensure supervision through documented procedures and oversight.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_6.description: This control requires that maintenance on cloud systems is performed in a timely manner to address vulnerabilities and ensure operational integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MA_6.remediation: Establish a maintenance schedule based on risk assessments and vendor recommendations. Use automated patch management tools to apply updates promptly and document all maintenance actions to track compliance and timelines.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_1.description: This control requires the establishment of policies and procedures for managing media used in cloud environments, including storage, transport, and sanitization.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_1.remediation: Develop media management policies that classify data sensitivity and define handling procedures. Train personnel on these policies and implement regular audits to ensure media is properly secured and disposed of according to standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_2.description: This control requires that access to media containing cloud data is restricted to authorized personnel to prevent unauthorized disclosure or modification.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_2.remediation: Implement access controls such as encryption and role-based permissions for media storage. Use logging to monitor access attempts and conduct periodic reviews to ensure only authorized individuals can access sensitive media.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_3.description: This control requires that media used in cloud systems is marked with appropriate labels to indicate handling and classification levels.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_3.remediation: Establish labeling standards for media based on data classification policies. Train staff to apply labels consistently and use automated tools where possible to enforce marking requirements for digital and physical media.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_4.description: This control requires that media storing cloud data is physically and logically secured to protect against theft, damage, or unauthorized access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_4.remediation: Store media in secure, access-controlled environments and use encryption for data at rest. Implement inventory management to track media locations and conduct regular inspections to ensure storage conditions meet security standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_5.description: This control requires that media transported within or outside cloud environments is protected to prevent loss, theft, or unauthorized access during transit.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_5.remediation: Use encrypted containers or secure couriers for physical media transport and employ encrypted connections for digital transfers. Maintain chain-of-custody logs and verify recipient authorization before releasing media.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_6.description: This control requires that media containing cloud data is properly sanitized or destroyed before disposal or reuse to prevent data leakage.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_6.remediation: Follow industry standards like NIST SP 800-88 for media sanitization, using methods such as degaussing, shredding, or secure wiping. Document sanitization processes and verify completion through audits to ensure no residual data remains.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_7.description: This control requires that media used in cloud systems is restricted to authorized purposes and monitored to prevent misuse.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_MP_7.remediation: Implement usage policies that define approved activities for media and enforce through access controls. Monitor media usage with logging tools and conduct regular reviews to detect and address any unauthorized or inappropriate use.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_1.description: This control requires the establishment of policies and procedures for physical and environmental protection of cloud infrastructure and facilities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_1.remediation: Develop comprehensive physical security policies covering access controls, environmental monitoring, and disaster recovery. Regularly update procedures based on risk assessments and ensure staff are trained to implement them effectively.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_12.description: This control requires that emergency lighting is provided in cloud facility areas to ensure safety and operational continuity during power failures.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_12.remediation: Install and maintain backup lighting systems, such as battery-powered or generator-supported lights, in critical areas. Test emergency lighting regularly and include it in facility maintenance schedules to ensure reliability during incidents.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_13.description: This control requires that fire protection systems are implemented in cloud facilities to detect and suppress fires, protecting equipment and data.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_13.remediation: Deploy fire detection and suppression systems, like smoke alarms and sprinklers, in data centers and server rooms. Conduct regular inspections and drills to ensure systems are functional and personnel know evacuation procedures.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_14.description: This control requires that environmental controls, such as temperature and humidity regulation, are maintained in cloud facilities to prevent hardware damage.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_14.remediation: Use HVAC systems to monitor and control environmental conditions in server rooms. Implement alerts for deviations and perform routine maintenance to ensure systems operate within specified ranges to protect cloud infrastructure.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_15.description: This control requires that measures are in place to protect cloud facilities from water damage, such as leaks or flooding.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_15.remediation: Install water detection sensors and drainage systems in critical areas. Regularly inspect plumbing and roofing for vulnerabilities, and develop response plans to mitigate water-related incidents quickly.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_16.description: This control requires that delivery and removal of equipment in cloud facilities are authorized and monitored to prevent unauthorized access or tampering.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_16.remediation: Establish procedures for vetting and escorting delivery personnel, and maintain logs of all equipment movements. Use surveillance and access controls to monitor entry points and ensure only approved items are brought in or out.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_2.description: This control requires that physical access to cloud facilities is authorized based on roles and responsibilities to prevent unauthorized entry.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_2.remediation: Implement access control systems, such as keycards or biometrics, and maintain an authorization list. Regularly review access permissions and revoke credentials for personnel who no longer require entry to secure areas.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_3.description: This control requires that physical access controls, such as locks and barriers, are used to secure cloud facility perimeters and sensitive areas.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_3.remediation: Deploy physical barriers like fences, gates, and mantraps, and use electronic locks on doors. Conduct periodic security assessments to identify and address vulnerabilities in access control measures.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_6.description: This control requires that physical access to cloud facilities is monitored through surveillance and logging to detect and respond to unauthorized activities.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_6.remediation: Install CCTV cameras and alarm systems at entry points and critical areas. Retain access logs and review them regularly for anomalies, and ensure monitoring feeds are monitored or recorded for incident investigation.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_8.description: This control requires that records of visitor access to cloud facilities are maintained to track and audit entry for security purposes.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PE_8.remediation: Implement a visitor management system that logs names, purposes, and entry/exit times. Require visitors to sign in and be escorted, and retain records for a specified period to support audits and investigations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_1.description: This control requires the establishment of policies and procedures for planning and managing cloud security activities and compliance.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_1.remediation: Develop security planning documents that outline objectives, roles, and compliance requirements. Regularly update policies based on changes in regulations or cloud environments and ensure they are communicated and enforced across the organization.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_2.description: This control requires the development and maintenance of system security and privacy plans that outline the security and privacy requirements, controls, and responsibilities for the system.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_2.remediation: Establish a documented process for creating and updating security and privacy plans, ensuring they align with organizational policies and regulatory requirements. Regularly review and approve these plans through governance committees, and integrate them into system lifecycle management.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_4.description: This control mandates the establishment of rules of behavior that define acceptable use and security responsibilities for individuals accessing the system.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_4.remediation: Develop and disseminate clear rules of behavior documents, requiring personnel to acknowledge and comply with them. Implement training sessions and periodic reviews to ensure adherence, and enforce consequences for violations through organizational policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_8.description: This control requires the design and implementation of security and privacy architectures that integrate protective measures into the system's structure.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PL_8.remediation: Define architectural standards and patterns that incorporate security and privacy by design, using frameworks like Zero Trust. Conduct regular architecture reviews and updates to address emerging threats, and ensure alignment with organizational risk management processes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_1.description: This control involves creating and maintaining personnel security policies and procedures to manage risks associated with human resources.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_1.remediation: Document comprehensive personnel security policies covering hiring, screening, and ongoing management. Train HR and security teams on these procedures, and establish audit mechanisms to verify compliance and effectiveness.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_2.description: This control requires assigning risk designations to positions based on the sensitivity of the roles and access levels.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_2.remediation: Develop a risk assessment framework to categorize positions by impact level, and integrate this into hiring and access control processes. Regularly review and update designations to reflect changes in roles or organizational structure.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_3.description: This control mandates conducting personnel screening processes to verify credentials and assess suitability for access to sensitive systems.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_3.remediation: Implement standardized screening procedures, including background checks and reference verifications, tailored to position risk levels. Maintain records of screenings and ensure periodic re-screening for high-risk roles.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_4.description: This control requires procedures for securely terminating personnel access to systems and assets upon departure.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_4.remediation: Establish a formal offboarding process that includes revoking all access rights, collecting organizational assets, and conducting exit interviews. Automate access revocation where possible using IAM tools, and verify completion through audit logs.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_5.description: This control involves managing personnel transfers by adjusting access and responsibilities to align with new roles.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_5.remediation: Define transfer procedures that include access review and modification based on the new position's requirements. Coordinate between HR and IT teams to ensure timely updates, and document changes for audit purposes.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_6.description: This control requires personnel to sign access agreements that outline security responsibilities and compliance expectations.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_6.remediation: Develop and implement access agreement templates that cover confidentiality, acceptable use, and incident reporting. Ensure all personnel sign these agreements upon onboarding and during role changes, and store them securely for compliance audits.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_7.description: This control mandates security measures for external personnel, such as contractors or vendors, accessing organizational systems.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_7.remediation: Establish vetting and monitoring processes for external personnel, including contract clauses for security compliance. Limit access to necessary resources only, and conduct regular reviews of external access privileges.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_8.description: This control involves applying sanctions or disciplinary actions for personnel who violate security policies.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_PS_8.remediation: Define clear sanction policies in personnel security procedures, and ensure consistent enforcement across the organization. Train managers on reporting violations and applying sanctions, and document incidents for accountability and improvement.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_1.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining risk assessment policies and procedures to identify and manage security risks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_1.remediation: Enable automated risk assessment tools like AWS GuardDuty, Azure Security Center, GCP Security Command Center, or OCI Cloud Guard. Configure these tools to continuously monitor for threats, and integrate findings into incident response and remediation workflows.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_2.description: This control involves categorizing systems based on security impact levels to guide protection measures.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_2.remediation: Develop a categorization framework using standards like NIST SP 800-60, and apply it to all systems during design and acquisition. Document categories in system inventories and use them to tailor security controls and monitoring efforts.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_3.description: This control mandates conducting regular risk assessments to evaluate threats, vulnerabilities, and impacts on organizational operations.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_3.remediation: Schedule periodic risk assessments using qualitative or quantitative methods, and involve stakeholders from IT, security, and business units. Document assessment results and action plans, and update risk registers to track mitigation progress.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_5.description: This control requires continuous vulnerability monitoring and scanning to detect and address security weaknesses in systems.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_RA_5.remediation: Deploy automated scanning tools such as AWS GuardDuty, Azure Security Center, GCP Security Command Center, or OCI Cloud Guard. Configure regular scans, prioritize vulnerabilities based on risk, and integrate with patch management processes for timely remediation.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_1.description: This control involves creating and maintaining system and services acquisition policies and procedures to ensure security integration.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_1.remediation: Document acquisition procedures that include security requirements, vendor assessments, and compliance checks. Train procurement teams on these policies, and conduct reviews of acquired systems before deployment to verify security controls.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_10.description: This control requires implementing configuration management processes for developers to maintain system integrity and security.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_10.remediation: Establish version control and change management practices, using tools like Git for code repositories. Enforce peer reviews and automated testing in CI/CD pipelines, and document configuration baselines to track changes and ensure consistency.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_11.description: This control mandates developer testing and evaluation activities to validate security and functionality before deployment.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_11.remediation: Integrate security testing, such as SAST and DAST, into the development lifecycle. Define testing criteria and success metrics, and require sign-off from security teams before releasing code to production environments.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_15.description: This control requires defining development processes, standards, and tools to ensure secure and consistent software creation.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_15.remediation: Adopt secure development frameworks and standards, such as OWASP SAMM, and provide developers with approved tools and libraries. Regularly update standards based on industry best practices, and conduct audits to ensure adherence across projects.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_16.description: This control involves providing security and privacy training to developers to enhance awareness and skills.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_16.remediation: Develop and deliver regular training programs on secure coding practices, threat modeling, and compliance requirements. Use hands-on workshops and online courses, and track completion to ensure all developers are adequately trained and updated.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_17.description: This control requires developers to incorporate security and privacy architecture and design principles into system development.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_17.remediation: Implement design reviews and threat modeling sessions early in the development lifecycle. Use architectural patterns that prioritize security, such as least privilege and encryption, and document design decisions for future reference and audits.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_2.description: This control involves allocating sufficient resources, including budget and personnel, to support system acquisition and security efforts.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_2.remediation: Integrate security resource requirements into project planning and budgeting processes. Advocate for dedicated security teams and tools, and regularly review resource allocations to address evolving threats and organizational needs.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_3.description: This control mandates following a system development life cycle (SDLC) that integrates security at each phase.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_3.remediation: Adopt a secure SDLC framework, such as Microsoft SDL or NIST SP 800-64, and enforce security gates at requirements, design, implementation, and deployment stages. Use automated tools to streamline compliance and document all security activities throughout the lifecycle.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_4.description: This control requires establishing an acquisition process that includes security assessments and vendor management.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_4.remediation: Develop procurement checklists that mandate security reviews, such as third-party audits and compliance certifications. Evaluate vendors based on security posture, and include security clauses in contracts to ensure ongoing adherence to standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_5.description: This control involves maintaining comprehensive system documentation, including security configurations and operational procedures.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_5.remediation: Create and update documentation repositories that cover system architecture, security controls, and incident response plans. Use tools like Confluence or GitHub Wikis, and ensure documentation is accessible to relevant teams and reviewed periodically for accuracy.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_8.description: This control requires integrating security and privacy principles into system engineering processes, ensuring that security is built into the design and development phases.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_8.remediation: Establish a formal process for security and privacy engineering reviews during system design and development. Use threat modeling and secure coding practices, and document these procedures in organizational policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_9.description: This control mandates managing and securing external system services to prevent unauthorized access and ensure data protection.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SA_9.remediation: Implement a vetting process for external services, including security assessments and contractual agreements. Monitor and audit external service usage regularly to ensure compliance with security policies.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_1.description: This control involves developing and maintaining policies and procedures for system security to guide implementation and compliance.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_1.remediation: Create and document comprehensive security policies and procedures, and review them annually. Train staff on these policies and ensure they are integrated into operational workflows.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_10.description: This control requires implementing network disconnect capabilities to terminate sessions or connections in response to security events.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_10.remediation: Configure network devices and security tools to support automatic disconnect features. Establish procedures for manual disconnection during incidents and test these capabilities regularly.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_12.description: This control ensures cryptographic keys are established and managed securely, including generation, distribution, storage, and rotation.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_12.remediation: Use managed key services like AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or GCP Cloud KMS with automatic key rotation enabled. Implement strict access controls and audit logging for key usage.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_13.description: This control mandates using cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality and integrity of information during transmission.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_13.remediation: Enable TLS/SSL encryption for all data in transit, such as using HTTPS for APIs and load balancers. Regularly update certificates and use strong cryptographic algorithms.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_15.description: This control involves securing collaborative computing devices and applications to prevent unauthorized access and data leakage.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_15.remediation: Implement access controls and monitoring for collaborative tools. Establish usage policies and conduct regular security training for users to mitigate risks.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_17.description: This control requires managing Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) certificates to ensure secure authentication and encryption.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_17.remediation: Use a centralized PKI management system for issuing, renewing, and revoking certificates. Monitor certificate expiration and enforce strong cryptographic standards.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_18.description: This control addresses the security risks associated with mobile code, such as scripts or applets, to prevent malicious execution.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_18.remediation: Implement policies to restrict or sandbox mobile code execution. Use security tools to scan and validate mobile code before deployment.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_2.description: This control ensures separation between system and user functionality to limit privileges and reduce attack surfaces.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_2.remediation: Configure systems with least privilege principles, using separate accounts for administrative and user tasks. Implement role-based access control (RBAC) to enforce separation.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_20.description: This control requires securing authoritative name/address resolution services to prevent DNS-based attacks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_20.remediation: Harden DNS servers with DNSSEC and access controls. Monitor DNS traffic for anomalies and ensure authoritative sources are protected from unauthorized modifications.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_21.description: This control involves securing recursive or caching name/address resolution services to ensure reliable and safe DNS queries.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_21.remediation: Configure recursive resolvers with security features like DNS filtering and rate limiting. Regularly update DNS software and audit resolver configurations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_22.description: This control mandates designing and provisioning name/address resolution services with security and availability in mind.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_22.remediation: Use deletion protection for critical resources like load balancers and databases to prevent accidental removal. Implement redundancy and monitor service health.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_23.description: This control ensures session authenticity to prevent hijacking or replay attacks during user interactions.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_23.remediation: Implement secure session management with tokens, timeouts, and encryption. Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) and monitor for unusual session activities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_28.description: This control requires protecting information at rest using encryption to prevent unauthorized access to stored data.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_28.remediation: Enable encryption for all storage services, such as S3 buckets, Azure Storage, or GCP Cloud Storage. Use customer-managed keys and enforce encryption policies via IaC.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_39.description: This control involves isolating processes to prevent interference and enhance system security.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_39.remediation: Use containerization or virtualization to separate processes. Implement security groups and network policies to restrict inter-process communication.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_4.description: This control ensures information in shared system resources is protected from unauthorized access or leakage.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_4.remediation: Implement access controls and encryption for shared resources like memory or storage. Regularly audit shared resource usage and apply security patches.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_5.description: This control requires implementing denial-of-service (DoS) protection mechanisms to maintain system availability.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_5.remediation: Enable security services like AWS GuardDuty, Azure Security Center, or GCP Security Command Center. Configure rate limiting and use DDoS protection services.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_7.description: This control involves protecting network boundaries to control traffic flow and prevent unauthorized access.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_7.remediation: Configure firewalls and security groups to restrict public access. Use network segmentation and monitor for misconfigurations like public snapshots.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_8.description: This control mandates ensuring transmission confidentiality and integrity through cryptographic protections.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SC_8.remediation: Enforce HTTPS redirects for load balancers and APIs. Use TLS encryption for all data in transit and regularly update security configurations.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_1.description: This control involves developing and maintaining policies and procedures for system integrity to prevent unauthorized changes.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_1.remediation: Create integrity policies and procedures, and implement change management processes. Use tools like checksums and audit logs to monitor for unauthorized modifications.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_10.description: This control requires validating information input to prevent injection attacks and ensure data integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_10.remediation: Implement input validation in applications using sanitization and whitelisting. Conduct regular security testing to identify and fix vulnerabilities.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_11.description: This control involves handling errors securely to avoid information leakage and system disruptions.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_11.remediation: Configure applications to log errors without exposing sensitive details. Use generic error messages and monitor error logs for security incidents.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_12.description: This control mandates managing and retaining information according to policies to ensure compliance and security.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_12.remediation: Establish data retention policies and use automated tools to enforce them. Regularly review and archive or delete data as per regulatory requirements.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_16.description: This control requires protecting memory from unauthorized access and corruption to maintain system integrity.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_16.remediation: Enable memory protection features in operating systems and applications. Use security tools like GuardDuty or Cloud Guard to detect memory-based threats.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_2.description: This control requires timely remediation of software flaws and vulnerabilities in cloud systems, including patching and configuration updates to address security weaknesses.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_2.remediation: Implement automated patch management using tools like AWS Systems Manager, Azure Update Management, or GCP OS Patch Management. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to enforce baseline configurations and integrate vulnerability scanning into CI/CD pipelines for continuous compliance.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_3.description: This control requires protection against malicious code by implementing antivirus, anti-malware, and intrusion detection systems across cloud environments.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_3.remediation: Establish a manual process to deploy and maintain endpoint protection solutions, such as AWS GuardDuty, Azure Defender, or GCP Security Command Center. Regularly update signatures and conduct security awareness training to handle incidents, ensuring procedures are documented and reviewed.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_4.description: This control requires continuous system monitoring to detect and respond to security events, using tools like SIEM and cloud-native monitoring services.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_4.remediation: Enable automated monitoring with AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, or GCP Logging, and set up alerts for suspicious activities. Use IaC to deploy monitoring configurations consistently and integrate with incident response workflows for rapid remediation.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_5.description: This control requires processes to receive, assess, and act on security alerts, advisories, and directives from external sources like vendors or government agencies.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_5.remediation: Create a manual procedure to subscribe to security feeds, assign responsibility for review, and update policies based on advisories. Document steps for dissemination and compliance verification, ensuring regular training and audits.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_7.description: This control requires ensuring the integrity of software, firmware, and information through measures like checksums, digital signatures, and change management.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_7.remediation: Implement manual processes for verifying integrity using hash functions and signed updates. Establish change control boards and documentation procedures, with regular audits to detect unauthorized modifications.

canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_8.description: This control requires protection against spam through email filtering, anti-phishing tools, and user education to reduce security risks.
canada_pbmm_moderate_CCCS_SI_8.remediation: Deploy manual spam protection solutions like AWS SES, Azure Exchange Online Protection, or third-party filters. Update filtering rules regularly and conduct user awareness programs, documenting configurations and incident responses.

cis_alicloud_1.1.10.description: Ensure that the Container Network Interface files have ownership set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.10.remediation: Use automated tools like Kubernetes admission controllers or IaC templates to enforce file ownership. Regularly audit with commands like 'chown root:root' and integrate checks into CI/CD pipelines to prevent unauthorized changes.

cis_alicloud_1.1.11.description: Ensure that the etcd data directory has permissions of 700 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.11.remediation: Manually set permissions using 'chmod 700' on the etcd directory and document the procedure. Conduct periodic audits and include this in security hardening checklists for Kubernetes clusters.

cis_alicloud_1.1.12.description: Ensure that the etcd data directory ownership is set to etcd:etcd.
cis_alicloud_1.1.12.remediation: Manually configure ownership with 'chown etcd:etcd' and verify through regular audits. Update deployment scripts and documentation to ensure consistency across environments.

cis_alicloud_1.1.13.description: Ensure that the admin.conf file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.13.remediation: Manually apply permissions using 'chmod 644' on admin.conf files and audit regularly. Include this step in Kubernetes setup guides and security compliance reviews.

cis_alicloud_1.1.14.description: Ensure that the admin.conf file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.14.remediation: Manually set ownership with 'chown root:root' and document the process. Perform routine checks and integrate into security hardening procedures for cluster maintenance.

cis_alicloud_1.1.15.description: Ensure that the scheduler.conf file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.15.remediation: Manually configure permissions using 'chmod 644' and verify through audits. Update deployment checklists and training materials to enforce this control consistently.

cis_alicloud_1.1.16.description: Ensure that the scheduler.conf file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.16.remediation: Manually set ownership with 'chown root:root' and include in security reviews. Document steps and conduct periodic validations to maintain compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.1.17.description: Ensure that the controller-manager.conf file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.17.remediation: Manually apply permissions using 'chmod 644' and audit regularly. Incorporate this into Kubernetes configuration management and security hardening protocols.

cis_alicloud_1.1.18.description: Ensure that the controller-manager.conf file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.18.remediation: Manually configure ownership with 'chown root:root' and verify through checks. Update documentation and training to ensure adherence during cluster deployments.

cis_alicloud_1.1.19.description: Ensure that the Kubernetes PKI directory and file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.19.remediation: Use automated scripts or IaC to enforce ownership across PKI files. Regularly scan with tools like kube-bench and integrate into CI/CD for continuous compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.1.2.description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.2.remediation: Manually set ownership using 'chown root:root' and document the procedure. Conduct periodic audits and include in security hardening checklists for Kubernetes.

cis_alicloud_1.1.20.description: Ensure that Kubernetes PKI certificate files have permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.20.remediation: Manually apply permissions with 'chmod 644' and audit regularly. Update security policies and include in routine maintenance procedures for certificate management.

cis_alicloud_1.1.21.description: Ensure that Kubernetes PKI key files have permissions of 600.
cis_alicloud_1.1.21.remediation: Automate permission settings using tools like Ansible or Terraform to enforce 'chmod 600'. Integrate checks into security scanning and monitor for deviations to protect sensitive keys.

cis_alicloud_1.1.3.description: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.3.remediation: Manually set permissions using 'chmod 644' and verify through audits. Include this in Kubernetes security guidelines and regular compliance reviews.

cis_alicloud_1.1.4.description: Ensure that the controller manager pod specification file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.4.remediation: Manually configure ownership with 'chown root:root' and document the process. Perform routine checks and update deployment procedures to ensure consistency.

cis_alicloud_1.1.5.description: Ensure that the scheduler pod specification file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.5.remediation: Manually apply permissions using 'chmod 644' and audit regularly. Incorporate into security hardening steps and training for Kubernetes administrators.

cis_alicloud_1.1.6.description: Ensure that the scheduler pod specification file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.6.remediation: Manually set ownership with 'chown root:root' and include in security reviews. Document steps and conduct periodic validations to maintain compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.1.7.description: Ensure that the /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.7.remediation: Manually configure permissions using 'chmod 644' and audit regularly. Update system hardening procedures and include in security compliance checklists.

cis_alicloud_1.1.8.description: Ensure that the /usr/lib/systemd/system/etcd.service file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_1.1.8.remediation: Manually set ownership with 'chown root:root' and verify through checks. Document the process and integrate into routine maintenance and security audits.

cis_alicloud_1.1.9.description: Ensure that the Container Network Interface files have permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_1.1.9.remediation: Use IaC tools like Terraform or Alibaba Cloud Resource Orchestration Service to set file permissions to 644 or stricter. Implement RBAC and security policies to enforce least privilege access, and regularly audit file permissions with CSPM tools to detect deviations.

cis_alicloud_1.10.description: RAM password policies can be used to ensure password complexity. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one number.
cis_alicloud_1.10.remediation: Configure the RAM password policy via Alibaba Cloud Console or SDK to enforce at least one numeric character. Use IaC templates to automate this setting across environments, and monitor compliance with CSPM scans to ensure adherence.

cis_alicloud_1.11.description: RAM password policies can be used to ensure password complexity. It is recommended that the password policy require a minimum of 14 or greater characters for any password.
cis_alicloud_1.11.remediation: Set the minimum password length to 14 or more in the RAM password policy using Alibaba Cloud management tools. Automate this configuration with IaC, and use CSPM to alert on any policy violations or weak password settings.

cis_alicloud_1.12.description: It is recommended that the password policy prevent the reuse of passwords.
cis_alicloud_1.12.remediation: Enable password reuse prevention in the RAM password policy, typically by setting a history limit (e.g., 24 previous passwords). Implement this via IaC for consistency, and use CSPM to verify that the policy is applied and enforced across all accounts.

cis_alicloud_1.13.description: RAM password policies can require passwords to be expired after a given number of days. It is recommended that the password policy expire passwords after 365 days or greater.
cis_alicloud_1.13.remediation: Configure the RAM password policy to set a maximum password age of 365 days or more. Use IaC to deploy this policy automatically, and leverage CSPM tools to monitor for expired passwords and ensure timely rotation.

cis_alicloud_1.14.description: RAM password policies can temporarily block logon after several incorrect logon attempts within an hour. It is recommended that the password policy is set to temporarily block logon after 5 incorrect logon attempts within an hour.
cis_alicloud_1.14.remediation: Set the login attempt limit to 5 within an hour in the RAM password policy. Automate this configuration with IaC, and use CSPM to detect and alert on any deviations or excessive login failures that could indicate brute-force attacks.

cis_alicloud_1.15.description: RAM policies represent permissions that can be granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended and considered a standard security advice to grant least privilege—that is, granting only the permissions required to perform tasks. Determine what users need to do and then create policies with permissions only fits those tasks, instead of allowing full administrative privileges.
cis_alicloud_1.15.remediation: Avoid creating RAM policies with full "*:*" administrative privileges. Use IaC to define least-privilege policies, implement RBAC to assign permissions to groups or roles, and regularly audit policies with CSPM to identify and remove overly permissive rules.

cis_alicloud_1.16.description: By default, RAM users, groups, and roles have no access to Alibaba Cloud resources. RAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended that RAM policies be applied directly to groups and roles but not users.
cis_alicloud_1.16.remediation: Attach RAM policies only to groups or roles, not individual users. Use IaC to manage group and role assignments, enforce RBAC principles, and utilize CSPM to scan for direct user policy attachments and remediate them promptly.

cis_alicloud_1.2.description: Access keys provide programmatic access to a given Alibaba Cloud account. It is recommended that all access keys associated with the root account be removed.
cis_alicloud_1.2.remediation: Delete any access keys linked to the root account via the Alibaba Cloud Console or CLI. Implement IAM policies to prevent root key creation, use CSPM to continuously monitor for root access keys, and enforce key management best practices with encryption for stored credentials.

cis_alicloud_1.2.1.remediation: Establish a procedure to disable basic authentication in Kubernetes configurations. Use secure authentication methods like TLS certificates or OIDC, document the process in security policies, and conduct regular manual reviews to ensure compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.2.10.remediation: Define a process to enable the AlwaysPullImages admission control plugin in Kubernetes. Update cluster configurations manually, enforce image pull policies through security guidelines, and schedule periodic audits to verify plugin activation.
