cis_alicloud_1.2.11.description: The SecurityContextDeny admission controller can be used to deny pods which make use of some SecurityContext fields which could allow for privilege escalation in the cluster. This should be used where PodSecurityPolicy is not in place within the cluster.
cis_alicloud_1.2.11.remediation: Implement a procedure to set the SecurityContextDeny admission control plugin when PodSecurityPolicy is not used. Manually configure Kubernetes admission controllers, document the setup in runbooks, and review configurations regularly to maintain security.

cis_alicloud_1.2.12.remediation: Create a process to enable the ServiceAccount admission control plugin in Kubernetes. Use manual configuration steps to automate service account creation and management, update security policies accordingly, and perform routine checks to ensure proper plugin usage.

cis_alicloud_1.2.13.description: Reject creating objects in a namespace that is undergoing termination.
cis_alicloud_1.2.13.remediation: Establish a procedure to enable the NamespaceLifecycle admission control plugin. Manually configure Kubernetes to reject object creation in terminating namespaces, document this in operational guidelines, and conduct periodic validations to confirm plugin effectiveness.

cis_alicloud_1.2.14.description: Reject creating pods that do not match Pod Security Policies.
cis_alicloud_1.2.14.remediation: Define a process to enable the PodSecurityPolicy admission control plugin. Manually set up and enforce pod security policies in Kubernetes, update security documentation, and schedule regular reviews to ensure policies are applied and effective.

cis_alicloud_1.2.15.description: Limit the Node and Pod objects that a kubelet could modify.
cis_alicloud_1.2.15.remediation: Implement a procedure to enable the NodeRestriction admission control plugin. Manually configure Kubernetes to restrict kubelet permissions, document the configuration steps, and perform routine audits to verify that the plugin is active and functioning correctly.

cis_alicloud_1.2.16.description: Do not bind the insecure API service.
cis_alicloud_1.2.16.remediation: Establish a process to ensure the --insecure-bind-address argument is not set in Kubernetes configurations. Use manual checks and security policies to disable insecure binding, document the procedure, and conduct regular reviews to prevent exposure.

cis_alicloud_1.2.17.description: Do not bind to insecure port.
cis_alicloud_1.2.17.remediation: Define a procedure to set the --insecure-port argument to 0 in Kubernetes. Manually update API server configurations to disable insecure ports, enforce this through security guidelines, and schedule periodic audits to ensure compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.2.18.description: Do not disable the secure port.
cis_alicloud_1.2.18.remediation: Create a process to ensure the --secure-port argument is not set to 0 in Kubernetes. Use manual configuration steps to enable secure ports, document the setup in security policies, and review settings regularly to maintain secure API access.

cis_alicloud_1.2.19.remediation: Establish a procedure to set the --profiling argument to false in Kubernetes configurations. Manually disable profiling where unnecessary, update operational documentation, and conduct occasional checks to ensure profiling is turned off to reduce attack surface.

cis_alicloud_1.2.2.description: Do not use token based authentication.
cis_alicloud_1.2.2.remediation: Define a process to avoid setting the --token-auth-file parameter in Kubernetes. Use secure authentication methods like certificates, document the prohibition in security policies, and perform manual reviews to ensure token-based auth is not configured.

cis_alicloud_1.2.20.description: Enable auditing on the Kubernetes API Server and set the desired audit log path.
cis_alicloud_1.2.20.remediation: Configure the --audit-log-path argument in Kubernetes API server settings using IaC tools like Terraform. Enable audit logging to a secure location, use CSPM to monitor log integrity, and enforce encryption for audit logs at rest and in transit.

cis_alicloud_1.2.21.description: Retain the logs for at least 30 days or as appropriate.
cis_alicloud_1.2.21.remediation: Set the --audit-log-maxage argument to 30 or more in Kubernetes configurations via IaC. Implement log retention policies, use CSPM to verify retention periods, and ensure logs are stored securely with encryption to meet compliance requirements.

cis_alicloud_1.2.22.description: Retain 10 or an appropriate number of old log files.
cis_alicloud_1.2.22.remediation: Configure the --audit-log-maxbackup argument to 10 or as needed using IaC automation. Manage log file backups with rotation policies, leverage CSPM to audit backup counts, and protect backups with encryption to prevent data loss.

cis_alicloud_1.2.23.description: Rotate log files on reaching 100 MB or as appropriate.
cis_alicloud_1.2.23.remediation: Set the --audit-log-maxsize argument to 100 MB or suitable value via IaC tools. Implement log rotation mechanisms, use CSPM to monitor file sizes, and ensure rotated logs are encrypted and stored securely for audit and forensic purposes.

cis_alicloud_1.2.24.description: Set global request timeout for API server requests as appropriate.
cis_alicloud_1.2.24.remediation: Establish a procedure to review and set the --request-timeout argument in the API server configuration to prevent denial-of-service attacks. Document the timeout value and ensure it aligns with organizational performance and security requirements, updating it as needed during regular audits.

cis_alicloud_1.2.25.description: Validate service account before validating token.
cis_alicloud_1.2.25.remediation: Implement a process to configure the --service-account-lookup argument to true in the API server settings. This ensures service accounts are verified before token validation, enhancing security by preventing unauthorized access; verify this configuration during deployment and maintenance checks.

cis_alicloud_1.2.26.description: Explicitly set a service account public key file for service accounts on the apiserver.
cis_alicloud_1.2.26.remediation: Define a procedure to set the --service-account-key-file argument appropriately in the API server configuration. Use secure key management practices to generate and store the public key file, and regularly review this setting to ensure it remains valid and secure.

cis_alicloud_1.2.27.description: etcd should be configured to make use of TLS encryption for client connections.
cis_alicloud_1.2.27.remediation: Establish a manual process to configure the --etcd-certfile and --etcd-keyfile arguments with valid TLS certificates for etcd client connections. Ensure certificates are issued by a trusted CA, stored securely, and rotated periodically to maintain encryption integrity.

cis_alicloud_1.2.28.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
cis_alicloud_1.2.28.remediation: Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or Kubernetes manifests to set the --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments with valid certificates. Enable automated checks to verify TLS configuration and ensure certificates are encrypted and rotated regularly.

cis_alicloud_1.2.29.description: Setup TLS connection on the API server.
cis_alicloud_1.2.29.remediation: Automate the configuration of the --client-ca-file argument using IaC to specify a CA bundle for client certificate authentication. Implement RBAC policies to restrict access and use automated monitoring to ensure the file is present and up-to-date.

cis_alicloud_1.2.3.remediation: Create a procedure to set the --kubelet-https argument to true in the API server configuration. Ensure TLS certificates are properly configured for kubelet communications and validate this setting during security audits to prevent unencrypted data in transit.

cis_alicloud_1.2.30.description: etcd should be configured to make use of TLS encryption for client connections.
cis_alicloud_1.2.30.remediation: Define a manual process to set the --etcd-cafile argument with a trusted CA certificate for etcd TLS connections. Regularly audit the CA file to ensure it is valid and not expired, maintaining secure client authentication.

cis_alicloud_1.2.31.remediation: Automate the configuration of the --encryption-provider-config argument using IaC to enable encryption at rest for etcd. Use cloud KMS services for key management and implement automated checks to verify encryption is active and keys are rotated.

cis_alicloud_1.2.32.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
cis_alicloud_1.2.32.remediation: Use automated tools to configure encryption providers such as AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or GCP KMS for etcd encryption. Implement IaC to define provider settings and enable continuous monitoring to ensure encryption keys are properly managed and rotated.

cis_alicloud_1.2.33.description: Ensure that the API server is configured to only use strong cryptographic ciphers.
cis_alicloud_1.2.33.remediation: Establish a procedure to review and set the API server's cipher suites to exclude weak algorithms. Document the approved cipher list and verify configuration during deployment and updates to prevent cryptographic vulnerabilities.

cis_alicloud_1.2.4.remediation: Implement a manual process to configure the --kubelet-client-certificate and --kubelet-client-key arguments with valid TLS certificates. Ensure certificates are issued by a trusted CA and stored securely, with regular audits to maintain authentication integrity.

cis_alicloud_1.2.5.description: Do not always authorize all requests.
cis_alicloud_1.2.5.remediation: Automate the configuration to ensure the --authorization-mode argument is not set to AlwaysAllow. Use IaC to define proper authorization modes like RBAC and Node, and implement automated checks to enforce least privilege access policies.

cis_alicloud_1.2.6.description: Restrict kubelet nodes to reading only objects associated with them.
cis_alicloud_1.2.6.remediation: Use automated tools to include Node in the --authorization-mode argument. Configure RBAC policies to limit node permissions and enable continuous monitoring to ensure nodes cannot access unauthorized resources.

cis_alicloud_1.2.7.description: Turn on Role Based Access Control.
cis_alicloud_1.2.7.remediation: Automate the inclusion of RBAC in the --authorization-mode argument using IaC. Define and enforce role-based policies to restrict access, and use automated checks to verify RBAC is active and properly configured.

cis_alicloud_1.2.8.description: Limit the rate at which the API server accepts requests.
cis_alicloud_1.2.8.remediation: Establish a procedure to set the EventRateLimit admission control plugin in the API server configuration. Document rate limits based on organizational needs and review settings periodically to prevent denial-of-service attacks.

cis_alicloud_1.2.9.remediation: Create a process to ensure the AlwaysAdmit admission control plugin is not set in the API server configuration. Verify this during security audits and use documentation to enforce proper admission controls that require authorization.

cis_alicloud_1.3.description: With MFA enabled, anytime the “root” account logs on to Alibaba Cloud, it will be prompted for username and password followed by an authentication code from the virtual MFA device. It is recommended that MFA be enabled for the “root” user.
cis_alicloud_1.3.remediation: Automate the enforcement of MFA for the root account using Alibaba Cloud IAM policies. Enable virtual MFA devices and use automated checks to ensure MFA is active, with alerts for any non-compliant configurations.

cis_alicloud_1.3.1.description: Activate garbage collector on pod termination, as appropriate.
cis_alicloud_1.3.1.remediation: Define a procedure to set the --terminated-pod-gc-threshold argument in the controller manager configuration. Adjust the threshold based on cluster usage to manage resources efficiently and review during performance audits.

cis_alicloud_1.3.2.remediation: Establish a process to set the --profiling argument to false in the controller manager configuration unless required for debugging. Document the justification for any exceptions and verify this setting during security reviews.

cis_alicloud_1.3.3.description: Use individual service account credentials for each controller.
cis_alicloud_1.3.3.remediation: Implement a manual procedure to set the --use-service-account-credentials argument to true in the controller manager. Ensure each controller has distinct service accounts and audit permissions regularly to enforce least privilege.

cis_alicloud_1.3.4.description: Explicitly set a service account private key file for service accounts on the controller manager.
cis_alicloud_1.3.4.remediation: Define a process to configure the --service-account-private-key-file argument with a secure private key. Use key management best practices for storage and rotation, and validate this configuration during deployment checks.

cis_alicloud_1.3.5.description: Allow pods to verify the API server's serving certificate before establishing connections.
cis_alicloud_1.3.5.remediation: Establish a manual procedure to set the --root-ca-file argument with a trusted CA certificate in the controller manager. Ensure the CA file is valid and regularly updated to maintain secure pod-to-API server communications.

cis_alicloud_1.3.6.description: Do not bind the Controller Manager service to non-loopback insecure addresses.
cis_alicloud_1.3.6.remediation: Create a process to configure the --bind-address argument to 127.0.0.1 in the controller manager. Verify this setting prevents exposure to external networks and audit it during security assessments to block unauthorized access.

cis_alicloud_1.4.description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user logs on to Alibaba Cloud, they will be prompted for their user name and password followed by an authentication code from their virtual MFA device. It is recommended that MFA be enabled for all users that have a console password.
cis_alicloud_1.4.remediation: Automate the enforcement of MFA for all RAM users with console passwords using Alibaba Cloud IAM policies. Enable virtual MFA devices and use automated checks to ensure compliance, with notifications for users who have not configured MFA.

cis_alicloud_1.4.1.remediation: Review and update the scheduler service configuration to set the --profiling argument to false. This should be done manually by editing configuration files or using management tools, ensuring no unnecessary profiling data is collected to reduce attack surface.

cis_alicloud_1.4.2.description: Do not bind the scheduler service to non-loopback insecure addresses.
cis_alicloud_1.4.2.remediation: Manually configure the scheduler service to bind only to 127.0.0.1 by setting the --bind-address argument. This prevents exposure to external networks and should be verified through regular configuration audits.

cis_alicloud_1.5.description: Alibaba Cloud RAM users can logon to Alibaba Cloud console by using their user name and password. If a user has not logged on for 90 days or longer, it is recommended to disable the console access of the user.
cis_alicloud_1.5.remediation: Automate the detection of inactive users using Alibaba Cloud RAM APIs or CSPM tools. Set up policies to automatically disable console access after 90 days of inactivity, and regularly review user activity logs.

cis_alicloud_1.6.description: An access key consists of an access key ID and a secret, which are used to sign programmatic requests that you make to Alibaba Cloud. RAM users need their own access keys to make programmatic calls to Alibaba Cloud from the Alibaba Cloud SDKs, CLIs, or direct HTTP/HTTPS calls using the APIs for individual Alibaba Cloud services. It is recommended that all access keys be regularly rotated.
cis_alicloud_1.6.remediation: Implement automated key rotation policies using Alibaba Cloud RAM or third-party tools. Enforce rotation every 90 days, monitor key usage, and use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to manage access keys securely.

cis_alicloud_1.7.description: RAM password policies can be used to ensure password complexity. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one uppercase letter.
cis_alicloud_1.7.remediation: Configure the RAM password policy via Alibaba Cloud Console or APIs to enforce at least one uppercase letter. Use IaC templates like Terraform to automate this setting across all accounts and regularly audit compliance.

cis_alicloud_1.8.description: RAM password policies can be used to ensure password complexity. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one lowercase letter.
cis_alicloud_1.8.remediation: Set the RAM password policy to require at least one lowercase letter using Alibaba Cloud management tools. Automate this with scripts or IaC to ensure consistency and perform periodic checks for adherence.

cis_alicloud_1.9.description: RAM password policies can be used to ensure password complexity. It is recommended that the password policy require at least one symbol.
cis_alicloud_1.9.remediation: Update the RAM password policy to mandate at least one symbol through the Alibaba Cloud Console or API calls. Utilize automation tools to deploy this policy across environments and validate with regular security scans.

cis_alicloud_2.1.description: ActionTrail is a web service that records API calls for your account and delivers log files to you. The recorded information includes the identity of the API caller, the time of the API call, the source IP address of the API caller, the request parameters, and the response elements returned by the Alibaba Cloud service. ActionTrail provides a history of API calls for an account, including API calls made via the Management Console, SDKs, command line tools.
cis_alicloud_2.1.remediation: Enable ActionTrail logging for all regions and services in Alibaba Cloud. Configure it to export logs to a secure OSS bucket or Log Service, and use IaC to automate this setup for comprehensive audit coverage.

cis_alicloud_2.10.description: It is recommended that a query and alarm should be established for RAM Role creation, deletion and updating activities.
cis_alicloud_2.10.remediation: Set up log monitoring in Alibaba Cloud Log Service to track RAM Role changes. Create alerts for any creation, deletion, or update events, and automate this using CloudMonitor or similar tools for real-time notifications.

cis_alicloud_2.11.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Cloud Firewall rule changes.
cis_alicloud_2.11.remediation: Configure CloudMonitor to monitor Cloud Firewall rule modifications. Establish metric filters and alarms for any changes, and automate this process through Alibaba Cloud APIs or CSPM platforms to ensure prompt detection.

cis_alicloud_2.12.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for VPC network route changes.
cis_alicloud_2.12.remediation: Enable ActionTrail logging for VPC route changes and set up alerts in CloudMonitor. Use automation scripts or IaC to deploy these monitoring rules across all VPCs for consistent security oversight.

cis_alicloud_2.13.description: It is recommended that a log search/analysis query and alarm be established for VPC changes.
cis_alicloud_2.13.remediation: Integrate ActionTrail with Log Service to monitor VPC configuration changes. Create queries and alarms for any modifications, and automate this setup using Alibaba Cloud tools to maintain continuous monitoring.

cis_alicloud_2.14.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for OSS Bucket RAM changes.
cis_alicloud_2.14.remediation: Configure CloudMonitor to track OSS bucket permission changes. Set up metric filters and alerts for any RAM policy updates, and automate this with Alibaba Cloud services to enhance data protection.

cis_alicloud_2.15.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for RDS Instance configuration changes.
cis_alicloud_2.15.remediation: Enable ActionTrail logging for RDS instances and set up monitoring in CloudMonitor. Create alerts for configuration changes, and use automation tools to ensure this is applied across all RDS databases.

cis_alicloud_2.16.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to LogService and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for unauthorized API calls.
cis_alicloud_2.16.remediation: Direct ActionTrail logs to Log Service and configure queries to detect unauthorized API calls. Set up alarms in CloudMonitor for immediate alerts, and automate this process using IaC for consistent enforcement.

cis_alicloud_2.17.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by multi-factor authentication (MFA).
cis_alicloud_2.17.remediation: Use Log Service to monitor ActionTrail logs for console sign-ins without MFA. Configure alerts in CloudMonitor, and automate this setup to enforce MFA policies and reduce authentication risks.

cis_alicloud_2.18.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by root login attempts.
cis_alicloud_2.18.remediation: Monitor ActionTrail logs for root account usage via Log Service. Set up immediate alerts in CloudMonitor for any root login attempts, and automate this configuration to prevent unauthorized administrative access.

cis_alicloud_2.19.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for failed console authentication attempts.
cis_alicloud_2.19.remediation: Configure Log Service to track failed console sign-ins from ActionTrail logs. Establish alerts in CloudMonitor for multiple failures, and automate this monitoring to detect potential brute-force attacks.

cis_alicloud_2.2.description: ActionTrail logs a record of every API call made in your Alibaba Cloud account. These logs file are stored in an OSS bucket. It is recommended that the access control list (ACL) of the OSS bucket, which ActionTrail logs to, shall prevent public access to the ActionTrail logs.
cis_alicloud_2.2.remediation: Review and update the OSS bucket ACL to block public access for ActionTrail logs. Use Alibaba Cloud Console or APIs to enforce private settings, and automate this with IaC to ensure all log storage buckets are secure.

cis_alicloud_2.20.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for customer created KMSs which have changed state to disabled or deletion.
cis_alicloud_2.20.remediation: Set up Log Service to monitor ActionTrail logs for KMS key state changes. Configure alerts in CloudMonitor for disablement or deletion events, and automate this process to protect encryption keys.

cis_alicloud_2.21.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established for changes to OSS bucket policies.
cis_alicloud_2.21.remediation: Enable monitoring in Log Service for OSS bucket policy modifications via ActionTrail. Create alerts in CloudMonitor for any changes, and use automation tools to deploy this across all buckets for consistent oversight.

cis_alicloud_2.22.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing ActionTrail Logs to Log Service and establishing corresponding query and alarms. Security Groups are a stateful packet filter that controls ingress and egress traffic within a VPC. It is recommended that a query and alarm be established changes to Security Groups.
cis_alicloud_2.22.remediation: Configure Log Service to track security group changes from ActionTrail logs. Set up alerts in CloudMonitor for modifications, and automate this monitoring to maintain network security policies.

cis_alicloud_2.23.description: Ensure Activity Log Retention is set for 365 days or greater
cis_alicloud_2.23.remediation: Set the Logstore data retention period to at least 365 days in Alibaba Cloud Log Service. Use the console or APIs to configure this, and automate with IaC to ensure compliance across all log stores for long-term audit needs.

cis_alicloud_2.3.description: Log Service provides functions of log collection and analysis in real time across multiple cloud resources under the authorized resource owners. This enable the large-scale corporate for security governance over all resources owned by multiple accounts by integrating the log from different sources and monitoring. For example, Log Service supports the integration to collect logs from the following sources: • ActionTrail is a cloud service that records API calls made in a given Alibaba Cloud accou
cis_alicloud_2.3.remediation: Integrate audit logs from multiple cloud resources into Alibaba Cloud Log Service. Configure log sources like ActionTrail and enable automated collection, using IaC to deploy this integration for centralized monitoring and analysis.

cis_alicloud_2.4.description: Log Service shall be connected with Kubernetes clusters of Alibaba Cloud Container Service to collect the audit log for central monitoring and analysis. You can simply enable Log Service when creating a cluster for log collection.
cis_alicloud_2.4.remediation: Enable Log Service integration during the creation of Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes clusters. Use automation tools or IaC templates to ensure this is configured for all clusters, facilitating centralized log management.

cis_alicloud_2.5.description: The flow log can be used to capture the traffic of an Elastic Network Interface (ENI), Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) or Virtual Switch (VSwitch). The flow log of a VPC or VSwitch shall be integrated with Log Service to capture the traffic of all ENIs in the VPC or VSwtich including the ENIs created after the flow log function is enabled. The traffic data captured by flow logs is stored in Log Service for real-time monitoring and analysis. A capture window is about 10 minutes, during which the traf
cis_alicloud_2.5.remediation: Enable VPC flow logging via Alibaba Cloud Console or Terraform, ensuring integration with Log Service for all VPCs and VSwitches. Configure flow logs to capture both accepted and rejected traffic for comprehensive network monitoring. Use IaC tools like Terraform to automate deployment and maintain consistency across environments.

cis_alicloud_2.6.description: Alibaba Cloud Anti-DDoS Pro supports integration with Log Service for website access log (including HTTP flood attack logs) to enable the real-time analysis and reporting center features. The log collected can be monitored on a central dashboard on Log Service.
cis_alicloud_2.6.remediation: Enable Anti-DDoS log collection in the Alibaba Cloud Console under Anti-DDoS Pro settings, linking it to Log Service. Use Terraform or Alibaba Cloud SDKs to automate this configuration for all protected resources. Ensure logs are stored securely and reviewed regularly for attack detection.

cis_alicloud_2.7.description: Log Service collects log entries that record visits to and attacks on websites that are protected by Alibaba Cloud Web Application Firewall (WAF), and supports real-time log query and analysis. The query results are centrally displayed in dashboards.
cis_alicloud_2.7.remediation: Activate WAF log analysis in the Alibaba Cloud WAF console, ensuring integration with Log Service for all protected domains. Automate this using IaC tools like Terraform to apply consistently. Set up dashboards in Log Service for real-time monitoring and alerting on security events.

cis_alicloud_2.8.description: Log Service collects log entries of internet traffic that are protected by Cloud Firewall, and supports real-time log query and analysis. The query results are centrally displayed in dashboards.
cis_alicloud_2.8.remediation: Enable Cloud Firewall log analysis in the Alibaba Cloud Console under Cloud Firewall settings, connecting it to Log Service. Use automation scripts or Terraform to deploy this configuration across all firewall instances. Regularly review logs for unauthorized access attempts and adjust firewall rules as needed.

cis_alicloud_2.9.description: Log Service collects log entries of Security Center for security logs, network logs, and host logs, with 14 subtypes, including 1. Security logs a. Vulnerability logs b. Baseline logs c. Security alerting logs 2. Security logs a. Vulnerability logs b. Baseline logs c. Security alerting logs 3. Network logs a. DNS logs b. Local DNS logs c. Network session logs d. Web logs 4. Server logs a. Process initiation logs b. Network connection logs c. System logon logs d. Brute-force cracking logs e. Proc
cis_alicloud_2.9.remediation: Configure Security Center to send all log types (security, network, host) to Log Service via the Alibaba Cloud Console. Automate this setup using Terraform or Cloud SDKs to ensure comprehensive coverage. Set up alerts and dashboards in Log Service for proactive threat detection and incident response.

cis_alicloud_3.1.description: In order to prevent use of legacy networks, ECS instances should not have a legacy network configured.
cis_alicloud_3.1.remediation: Migrate all ECS instances from legacy networks to VPCs using the Alibaba Cloud Console or API calls. Update IaC templates like Terraform to use VPC configurations exclusively. Regularly audit ECS instances to detect and remediate any remaining legacy network attachments.

cis_alicloud_3.2.description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Alibaba Cloud resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 22 or port 3389.
cis_alicloud_3.2.remediation: Modify security group rules to restrict SSH (port 22) and RDP (port 3389) access to specific trusted IP ranges only. Use Terraform or Alibaba Cloud Console to enforce least privilege principles. Regularly review and update these rules to prevent exposure to the internet.

cis_alicloud_3.2.2.description: Ensure that the audit policy created for the cluster covers key security concerns.
cis_alicloud_3.2.2.remediation: Define and implement a comprehensive audit policy in Alibaba Cloud that logs all critical security events, such as authentication attempts and configuration changes. Use IaC tools to deploy this policy consistently across clusters. Regularly review audit logs to ensure coverage and compliance with security standards.

cis_alicloud_3.3.description: You can use the flow log function to monitor the IP traffic information for an ENI, a VSwitch or a VPC. If you create a flow log for a VSwitch or a VPC, all the Elastic Network Interfaces, including the newly created Elastic Network Interfaces, are monitored. Such flow log data is stored in Log Service, where you can view and analyze IP traffic information. It is recommended that VPC Flow Logs be enabled for packet "Rejects" for VPCs.
cis_alicloud_3.3.remediation: Enable VPC flow logging for all VPCs, specifically configuring it to capture rejected packets for security analysis. Use Terraform or Alibaba Cloud SDKs to automate this across all network resources. Integrate flow logs with Log Service for centralized monitoring and alerting on suspicious traffic.

cis_alicloud_3.4.description: Once a VPC peering connection is established, routing tables must be updated to establish any connections between the peered VPCs. These routes can be as specific as desired, even peering a VPC to only a single host on the other side of the connection.
cis_alicloud_3.4.remediation: Configure routing tables for VPC peering with least privilege access, specifying only necessary IP ranges or hosts. Use Terraform to define these routes in code for consistency and auditability. Regularly review peering connections to ensure routes are minimal and aligned with security requirements.

cis_alicloud_3.5.description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Alibaba Cloud resources. It is recommended that all security group configured with fine grained rules.
cis_alicloud_3.5.remediation: Define security group rules with specific IP addresses, ports, and protocols instead of using broad ranges like 0.0.0.0/0. Use Terraform or Alibaba Cloud Console to enforce these fine-grained rules. Conduct regular audits to remove overly permissive rules and maintain least privilege access.

cis_alicloud_4.1.description: Ensure that unattached disks in a subscription are encrypted.
cis_alicloud_4.1.remediation: Enable encryption for all unattached disks using Alibaba Cloud Key Management Service (KMS) or server-side encryption. Automate this via Terraform or Alibaba Cloud SDKs to apply encryption policies during disk creation. Regularly scan for and encrypt any unencrypted unattached disks to protect data at rest.

cis_alicloud_4.1.1.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_4.1.1.remediation: Manually check and set the kubelet service file permissions to 644 or more restrictive on all Kubernetes nodes using commands like chmod. Document this procedure in security policies and conduct regular audits. Use configuration management tools like Ansible to automate permission settings across clusters.

cis_alicloud_4.1.10.description: Ensure that if the kubelet refers to a configuration file with the --config argument, that file is owned by root:root.
cis_alicloud_4.1.10.remediation: Manually verify and set ownership of the kubelet configuration file to root:root using chown commands on all nodes. Include this check in security audit scripts and update documentation. Use automated tools like Puppet or Chef to enforce ownership policies consistently.

cis_alicloud_4.1.2.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_4.1.2.remediation: Manually set the ownership of the kubelet service file to root:root on each Kubernetes node using chown. Document this step in security procedures and perform regular compliance checks. Implement configuration management to automate ownership enforcement across the environment.

cis_alicloud_4.1.3.description: If kube-proxy is running, and if it is using a file-based kubeconfig file, ensure that the proxy kubeconfig file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_4.1.3.remediation: Manually check and adjust permissions of the kube-proxy kubeconfig file to 644 or more restrictive on all relevant nodes. Update security policies to include this requirement and conduct periodic audits. Use automation scripts to apply permission settings during node provisioning.

cis_alicloud_4.1.4.description: If kube-proxy is running, ensure that the file ownership of its kubeconfig file is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_4.1.4.remediation: Manually verify and set ownership of the kube-proxy kubeconfig file to root:root using chown commands. Incorporate this into security checklists and audit processes. Leverage configuration management tools to ensure consistent ownership across all Kubernetes nodes.

cis_alicloud_4.1.5.description: Ensure that the kubelet.conf file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_4.1.5.remediation: Manually set permissions of the kubelet.conf file to 644 or more restrictive on each Kubernetes node with chmod. Document this in security guidelines and perform regular compliance scans. Use automation to enforce these permissions during cluster setup and maintenance.

cis_alicloud_4.1.6.description: Ensure that the kubelet.conf file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_4.1.6.remediation: Manually adjust ownership of the kubelet.conf file to root:root using chown on all nodes. Update security procedures to include this control and conduct audits. Implement configuration management to automate ownership settings and ensure consistency.

cis_alicloud_4.1.7.description: Ensure that the certificate authorities file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_4.1.7.remediation: Manually check and set permissions of the certificate authorities file to 644 or more restrictive using chmod. Include this in security policy documentation and regular audit checks. Use tools like Ansible to automate permission management across all systems.

cis_alicloud_4.1.8.description: Ensure that the certificate authorities file ownership is set to root:root.
cis_alicloud_4.1.8.remediation: Manually verify and set ownership of the certificate authorities file to root:root with chown commands. Document this requirement in security standards and perform periodic reviews. Employ configuration management to enforce ownership policies automatically.

cis_alicloud_4.1.9.description: Ensure that if the kubelet refers to a configuration file with the --config argument, that file has permissions of 644 or more restrictive.
cis_alicloud_4.1.9.remediation: Manually adjust permissions of the kubelet configuration file referenced by --config to 644 or more restrictive using chmod. Update security checklists to include this step and conduct regular audits. Use automation scripts to apply permissions during node configuration.

cis_alicloud_4.2.description: Ensure that disk are encrypted when it is created with the creation of VM instance.
cis_alicloud_4.2.remediation: Enable encryption for all VM disks at creation using Alibaba Cloud KMS or server-side encryption options. Automate this via Terraform or Alibaba Cloud SDKs to ensure encryption is applied by default. Regularly audit VM instances to confirm disk encryption and remediate any unencrypted disks.

cis_alicloud_4.2.1.description: Disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server.
cis_alicloud_4.2.1.remediation: Manually configure the kubelet --anonymous-auth argument to false on all Kubernetes nodes by editing the kubelet configuration file. Document this setting in security policies and verify through audits. Use configuration management tools to enforce this setting across the cluster to prevent unauthorized access.

cis_alicloud_4.2.10.description: The RBAC role cluster-admin provides wide-ranging powers over the environment and should be used only where and when needed.
cis_alicloud_4.2.10.remediation: Manually review and restrict the use of cluster-admin RBAC roles to only necessary users and service accounts. Implement least privilege principles by creating custom roles with minimal permissions. Regularly audit RBAC assignments and update policies to reduce over-privileged access.

cis_alicloud_4.2.2.description: Do not allow all requests. Enable explicit authorization.
cis_alicloud_4.2.2.remediation: Configure the Kubernetes API server with explicit authorization modes like RBAC, Node, or Webhook instead of AlwaysAllow. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or Kubernetes manifests to enforce this setting, and regularly audit configurations to ensure compliance.

cis_alicloud_4.2.3.remediation: Manually set the --client-ca-file argument to a valid CA certificate file path in the Kubelet configuration. Establish a procedure for generating and rotating certificates, and document the steps in your security policy to ensure consistent enforcement across all nodes.

cis_alicloud_4.2.4.remediation: Manually configure the Kubelet by setting the --read-only-port argument to 0 in its configuration file. Implement a review process to verify this setting during node provisioning and updates, and use security policies to mandate disabling unnecessary ports.

cis_alicloud_4.2.5.description: Do not disable timeouts on streaming connections.
cis_alicloud_4.2.5.remediation: Manually set the --streaming-connection-idle-timeout argument to a non-zero value, such as 5m, in the Kubelet configuration. Define a standard timeout value in your operational procedures and conduct periodic audits to prevent denial-of-service risks from infinite connections.

cis_alicloud_4.2.6.description: Protect tuned kernel parameters from overriding kubelet default kernel parameter values.
cis_alicloud_4.2.6.remediation: Manually set the --protect-kernel-defaults argument to true in the Kubelet configuration. Establish a process for validating kernel settings during node setup and updates, and enforce this through security baselines to maintain system integrity.

cis_alicloud_4.2.7.remediation: Manually configure the Kubelet by setting the --make-iptables-util-chains argument to true. Document this requirement in your cluster hardening guide and perform regular checks to ensure Kubelet can properly manage network policies for pod communication.

cis_alicloud_4.2.8.description: Security relevant information should be captured. The --event-qps flag on the Kubelet can be used to limit the rate at which events are gathered. Setting this too low could result in relevant events not being logged, however the unlimited setting of 0 could result in a denial of service on the kubelet.
cis_alicloud_4.2.8.remediation: Manually set the --event-qps argument to a balanced value, such as 5, to capture essential events without overloading the Kubelet. Develop a monitoring procedure to adjust this based on cluster activity and log volume, ensuring critical security events are not missed.

cis_alicloud_4.2.9.description: Setup TLS connection on the Kubelets.
cis_alicloud_4.2.9.remediation: Configure the Kubelet with --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments pointing to valid TLS certificates. Use IaC to automate certificate deployment and rotation, and enforce encryption-in-transit policies to secure communications between nodes and the API server.

cis_alicloud_4.3.description: Security groups provide stateful filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Alibaba Cloud resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 22.
cis_alicloud_4.3.remediation: Modify security group rules to restrict SSH access (port 22) to specific trusted IP ranges only. Use IaC tools like Terraform to define and enforce these rules, and implement regular audits using CSPM tools to detect and remediate any open configurations.

cis_alicloud_4.4.description: Security groups provide filtering of ingress/egress network traffic to Aliyun resources. It is recommended that no security group allows unrestricted ingress access to port 3389.
cis_alicloud_4.4.remediation: Update security groups to limit RDP access (port 3389) to authorized IP addresses. Automate rule management with IaC, apply least-privilege network policies, and continuously monitor for deviations using cloud security scanners to prevent unauthorized remote access.

cis_alicloud_4.5.description: Ensure that the latest OS patches for all virtual machines are applied.
cis_alicloud_4.5.remediation: Implement automated patch management processes using tools like Alibaba Cloud Security Center or third-party solutions. Define a patch cadence in your security policy, schedule regular updates, and use IaC to enforce baseline images with the latest patches for new instances.

cis_alicloud_4.6.description: Install endpoint protection for all virtual machines.
cis_alicloud_4.6.remediation: Deploy endpoint protection agents, such as Alibaba Cloud Security Center agents, on all VMs. Use IaC templates to include agent installation during provisioning, monitor agent status through CSPM, and establish procedures for updating and maintaining protection software.

cis_alicloud_5.1.description: A bucket is a container used to store objects in Object Storage Service (OSS). All objects in OSS are stored in buckets. It is recommended that the access policy on OSS bucket does not allows anonymous and/or public access.
cis_alicloud_5.1.remediation: Configure OSS bucket policies to deny public access and restrict permissions to specific IAM roles or users. Use IaC to define secure bucket configurations, enable bucket encryption, and regularly audit policies with CSPM tools to detect and fix any public exposure.

cis_alicloud_5.1.2.description: The Kubernetes API stores secrets, which may be service account tokens for the Kubernetes API or credentials used by workloads in the cluster. Access to these secrets should be restricted to the smallest possible group of users to reduce the risk of privilege escalation.
cis_alicloud_5.1.2.remediation: Implement RBAC policies to limit secret access to only necessary users and service accounts. Use Kubernetes Role and RoleBinding resources with least-privilege principles, automate with IaC, and conduct periodic reviews of permissions to prevent unauthorized access.

cis_alicloud_5.1.3.description: Kubernetes Roles and ClusterRoles provide access to resources based on sets of objects and actions that can be taken on those objects. It is possible to set either of these to be the wildcard "*" which matches all items. Use of wildcards is not optimal from a security perspective as it may allow for inadvertent access to be granted when new resources are added to the Kubernetes API either as CRDs or in later versions of the product.
cis_alicloud_5.1.3.remediation: Define explicit resource and verb lists in Roles and ClusterRoles instead of using wildcards. Use IaC to manage these policies, enforce code reviews for RBAC changes, and regularly audit roles with CSPM tools to ensure least-privilege access.

cis_alicloud_5.1.4.description: The ability to create pods in a namespace can provide a number of opportunities for privilege escalation, such as assigning privileged service accounts to these pods or mounting hostPaths with access to sensitive data (unless Pod Security Policies are implemented to restrict this access) As such, access to create new pods should be restricted to the smallest possible group of users.
cis_alicloud_5.1.4.remediation: Restrict pod creation permissions using RBAC to only authorized users and service accounts. Implement Pod Security Policies or Pod Security Standards to limit pod capabilities, automate with IaC, and review permissions regularly to mitigate escalation risks.

cis_alicloud_5.1.5.description: The default service account should not be used to ensure that rights granted to applications can be more easily audited and reviewed.
cis_alicloud_5.1.5.remediation: Create and use dedicated service accounts for each application instead of the default service account. Use IaC to define service accounts with specific permissions, disable auto-mounting of default tokens, and audit service account usage to improve accountability.

cis_alicloud_5.1.6.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
cis_alicloud_5.1.6.remediation: Configure pods to disable automatic service account token mounting unless required. Use Kubernetes manifests to set automountServiceAccountToken: false, implement least-privilege access for tokens, and review pod specifications to minimize unnecessary API server communication.

cis_alicloud_5.2.description: A bucket is a container used to store objects in Object Storage Service (OSS). All objects in OSS are stored in buckets. It is recommended that storage object ACL should not grant public access.
cis_alicloud_5.2.remediation: Set object ACLs to private and use bucket policies to restrict access. Implement IaC to enforce these settings, enable server-side encryption for objects, and use CSPM scans to detect and remediate any publicly accessible objects in storage buckets.

cis_alicloud_5.2.1.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the securityContext.privileged flag set to true.
cis_alicloud_5.2.1.remediation: Enforce Pod Security Policies or Pod Security Standards to block privileged containers. Use IaC to define and apply these policies across clusters, conduct regular audits with CSPM tools, and restrict privileged access to only necessary workloads with strict approval processes.

cis_alicloud_5.2.2.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostPID flag set to true.
cis_alicloud_5.2.2.remediation: Implement Pod Security Policies or equivalent to prevent containers from sharing the host process ID namespace. Use IaC to enforce these settings, monitor pod configurations for compliance, and limit hostPID usage to isolated, approved workloads with documented justifications.

cis_alicloud_5.2.3.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostIPC flag set to true.
cis_alicloud_5.2.3.remediation: Manually configure Kubernetes to restrict containers from sharing the host IPC namespace by setting hostIPC to false in pod specs. Establish a review process for pod definitions, use security policies to mandate this restriction, and audit configurations regularly to prevent privilege escalation.

cis_alicloud_5.2.4.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostNetwork flag set to true.
cis_alicloud_5.2.4.remediation: Use Pod Security Policies or network policies to block containers from using the host network. Automate enforcement with IaC, isolate pod networking, and regularly scan for non-compliant pods using CSPM tools to reduce network-based attack surfaces.

cis_alicloud_5.2.5.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the allowPrivilegeEscalation flag set to true.
cis_alicloud_5.2.5.remediation: Configure Pod Security Policies or security contexts to set allowPrivilegeEscalation to false. Use IaC to apply these settings cluster-wide, implement runtime security monitoring, and review pod specs to prevent containers from gaining elevated privileges.

cis_alicloud_5.2.6.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run as the root user.
cis_alicloud_5.2.6.remediation: Enforce non-root user execution in containers using Pod Security Policies or security contexts. Use IaC to define runAsUser settings, implement image scanning to detect root usage, and regularly audit pod configurations to minimize security risks from privileged containers.

cis_alicloud_5.2.7.description: Do not generally permit containers with the potentially dangerous NET_RAW capability.
cis_alicloud_5.2.7.remediation: Use Pod Security Policies (PSPs) or Kubernetes Admission Controllers to block containers with the NET_RAW capability. In Infrastructure as Code (IaC), define policies that restrict this capability in pod specifications, and regularly audit container configurations to ensure compliance.

cis_alicloud_5.2.8.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities assigned beyond the default set.
cis_alicloud_5.2.8.remediation: Establish a manual review process for any container requiring additional capabilities. Use RBAC to restrict who can deploy containers with extra capabilities, and enforce policies through documentation and training to minimize exceptions.

cis_alicloud_5.2.9.description: There are a variety of CNI plugins available for Kubernetes. If the CNI in use does not support Network Policies it may not be possible to effectively restrict traffic in the cluster.
cis_alicloud_5.2.9.remediation: Manually assess and select a CNI plugin that supports Network Policies for your Kubernetes cluster. Update cluster configurations and documentation to ensure network traffic can be properly restricted, and train teams on implementing Network Policies.

cis_alicloud_5.3.description: OSS Bucket Access Logging generates a log that contains access records for each request made to your OSS bucket. An access log record contains details about the request, such as the request type, the resources specified in the request worked, and the time and date the request was processed. It is recommended that bucket access logging be enabled on the OSS bucket.
cis_alicloud_5.3.remediation: Enable access logging for all OSS buckets using Alibaba Cloud Console, CLI, or IaC tools like Terraform. Configure log storage in a secure bucket and set up monitoring alerts for unauthorized access attempts.

cis_alicloud_5.3.2.description: Kubernetes supports mounting secrets as data volumes or as environment variables. Minimize the use of environment variable secrets.
cis_alicloud_5.3.2.remediation: Define Network Policies for all Kubernetes namespaces using YAML manifests or Helm charts. Use tools like Calico or Cilium to enforce policies, and regularly audit namespaces to ensure no unprotected ones exist.

cis_alicloud_5.4.description: Enable the data encryption in transit.
cis_alicloud_5.4.remediation: Set 'Secure transfer required' to 'Enabled' for all storage accounts and services. Use IaC to enforce HTTPS-only policies, and configure load balancers or API gateways to redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS.

cis_alicloud_5.4.2.description: Configure Image Provenance for your deployment.
cis_alicloud_5.4.2.remediation: Integrate external secret management solutions like AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, or HashiCorp Vault. Use IaC to store secrets securely, enforce encryption, and implement RBAC to restrict access to authorized users and services.

cis_alicloud_5.5.description: Expire the shared URL signature within an hour.
cis_alicloud_5.5.remediation: Manually configure OSS bucket policies to set URL signature expiration to one hour or less. Establish procedures for generating temporary URLs and monitor usage logs to detect any overly permissive signatures.

cis_alicloud_5.6.description: URL signature is a URL that grants access rights to OSS. You can add signature information to a URL so that you can forward the URL to the third party for authorized access.A URL signature can be provided to the third party for authorized access.
cis_alicloud_5.6.remediation: Manually enforce that URL signatures are only allowed over HTTPS by configuring OSS settings. Update access policies and train teams on secure URL generation practices to prevent data exposure.

cis_alicloud_5.6.1.description: Use namespaces to isolate your Kubernetes objects.
cis_alicloud_5.6.1.remediation: Create and use namespaces in Kubernetes to segregate resources by environment or team. Define namespace policies in IaC, and implement RBAC to control access, ensuring administrative boundaries are maintained.

cis_alicloud_5.6.2.description: Enable runtime/default seccomp profile in your pod definitions.
cis_alicloud_5.6.2.remediation: Set the seccomp profile to runtime/default in all pod definitions using Kubernetes YAML or Helm charts. Use admission controllers to enforce this, and audit existing pods to update non-compliant configurations.

cis_alicloud_5.6.3.description: Apply Security Context to Your Pods and Containers
cis_alicloud_5.6.3.remediation: Define security contexts in pod and container specifications to set privileges, runAsUser, and capabilities. Use IaC to standardize these settings across deployments and implement policy-as-code tools for enforcement.

cis_alicloud_5.6.4.description: Kubernetes provides a default namespace, where objects are placed if no namespace is specified for them. Placing objects in this namespace makes application of RBAC and other controls more difficult.
cis_alicloud_5.6.4.remediation: Avoid using the default namespace by specifying namespaces in all Kubernetes resource definitions. Use IaC to enforce namespace usage and implement RBAC policies that restrict access to the default namespace.

cis_alicloud_5.7.description: Restricting default network access helps to provide a new layer of security, since OSS accept connections from clients on any network. To limit access to selected networks, the default action must be changed.
cis_alicloud_5.7.remediation: Configure OSS bucket network access rules to block public access. Use IaC to set policies that restrict access to specific VPCs or IP ranges, and regularly audit buckets to ensure no public exposure exists.

cis_alicloud_5.8.description: Enable server-side encryption (Encrypt with Service Key) for objects.
cis_alicloud_5.8.remediation: Enable server-side encryption with service-managed keys for all OSS buckets. Use Alibaba Cloud Console, CLI, or IaC to configure encryption settings, and ensure all new buckets are created with encryption enabled by default.

cis_alicloud_5.9.description: Enable server-side encryption (Encrypt with BYOK) for objects.
cis_alicloud_5.9.remediation: Enable server-side encryption with Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) using Alibaba Cloud KMS. Use IaC to integrate KMS keys with OSS buckets, and manage key rotation policies to maintain encryption security.

cis_alicloud_6.1.description: It is recommended to enforce all incoming connections to SQL database instance to use SSL.
cis_alicloud_6.1.remediation: Configure RDS instances to require SSL/TLS for all connections. Use Alibaba Cloud Console or IaC to enable SSL enforcement, and update application connection strings to use encrypted endpoints.

cis_alicloud_6.1.1.description: Scan images stored in Alibaba Cloud Container Registry (ACR) for vulnerabilities.
cis_alicloud_6.1.1.remediation: Enable image vulnerability scanning in ACR or integrate with third-party tools like Trivy or Clair. Use IaC to configure scanning policies and set up CI/CD pipelines to block deployments with critical vulnerabilities.

cis_alicloud_6.1.2.description: Restrict user access to ACR, limiting interaction with build images to only authorized personnel and service accounts.
cis_alicloud_6.1.2.remediation: Implement RBAC to limit ACR access to necessary users and service accounts. Use Alibaba Cloud RAM policies to define permissions, and audit access logs regularly to detect unauthorized activities.

cis_alicloud_6.1.3.description: Configure the Cluster Service Account with Storage Object Viewer Role to only allow read-only access to ACR.
cis_alicloud_6.1.3.remediation: Assign read-only roles to cluster service accounts for ACR access. Use Kubernetes service accounts and Alibaba Cloud RAM roles to enforce least privilege, and update IaC configurations to apply these settings automatically.

cis_alicloud_6.1.4.remediation: Restrict Kubernetes clusters to pull images only from approved registries. Use admission controllers or cluster policies to enforce this, and maintain a list of allowed registries in configuration management tools.

cis_alicloud_6.2.description: Database Server should accept connections only from trusted Network(s)/IP(s) and restrict access from the world.
cis_alicloud_6.2.remediation: Configure RDS instances to disable public access and restrict connections to specific VPCs or IP ranges. Use security groups and network ACLs in IaC to enforce these rules, and regularly audit network settings.

cis_alicloud_6.2.1.description: Encrypt Kubernetes secrets, stored in etcd, at the application-layer using a customer-managed key in Cloud KMS.
cis_alicloud_6.2.1.remediation: Enable encryption for Kubernetes secrets using Alibaba Cloud KMS. Configure the cluster to use customer-managed keys, and use IaC to automate key management and rotation policies.

cis_alicloud_6.3.description: Enable SQL auditing on all RDS except SQL Server 2012/2016/2017 and MariaDB TX.
cis_alicloud_6.3.remediation: Turn on auditing for applicable RDS instances via Alibaba Cloud Console or IaC. Configure audit logs to be stored securely and set up alerts for suspicious activities to enhance monitoring and compliance.

cis_alicloud_6.3.1.description: Enable Endpoint Private Access to restrict access to the cluster's control plane to only an allowlist of authorized IPs.
cis_alicloud_6.3.1.remediation: Configure private access for Kubernetes control plane endpoints and restrict IP allowlists. Use Alibaba Cloud networking settings and IaC to enforce these restrictions, and regularly update the allowlist based on access needs.

cis_alicloud_6.3.2.description: Disable access to the Kubernetes API from outside the node network if it is not required.
cis_alicloud_6.3.2.remediation: Enable private endpoint access and disable public access for ACK clusters via Alibaba Cloud Console or Terraform. Use network security groups to restrict inbound traffic to the Kubernetes API server from trusted IP ranges only.

cis_alicloud_6.3.3.description: Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses. Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses.
cis_alicloud_6.3.3.remediation: Configure ACK clusters to use private nodes by disabling public IP assignment during node creation. Use VPC peering or VPN for secure external access to private nodes.

cis_alicloud_6.3.4.description: Use Network Policy to restrict pod to pod traffic within a cluster and segregate workloads.
cis_alicloud_6.3.4.remediation: Enable and configure Network Policy in ACK clusters using Calico or other CNI plugins. Define policies to allow only necessary communication between pods based on labels and namespaces.

cis_alicloud_6.3.5.description: Encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers using TLS certificates.
cis_alicloud_6.3.5.remediation: Configure HTTPS listeners on ACK load balancers with valid TLS certificates from ACM or custom sources. Use Terraform to automate certificate deployment and enforce HTTPS-only traffic.

cis_alicloud_6.4.description: Database SQL Audit Retention should be configured to be greater than 90 days.
cis_alicloud_6.4.remediation: Set audit retention policies for Alibaba Cloud RDS instances to retain logs for at least 6 months via console or API. Use Log Service for long-term storage and compliance reporting.

cis_alicloud_6.4.1.description: Use Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) to encrypt the Alibaba Cloud Disk and attached it as data disk in ACK cluster.
cis_alicloud_6.4.1.remediation: Enable disk encryption with CMEK for EBS volumes in ACK clusters via KMS. Use Terraform to specify encryption keys during disk creation and attach encrypted disks to nodes.

cis_alicloud_6.5.description: Enable Transparent Data Encryption on every RDS instance.
cis_alicloud_6.5.remediation: Enable TDE for Alibaba Cloud RDS instances through the console or API. Use IaC tools like Terraform to enforce TDE on all new and existing database instances for data at rest encryption.

cis_alicloud_6.5.1.description: Send cluster audit logs to a remote aggregator to mitigate the risk of local tampering in the event of a breach.
cis_alicloud_6.5.1.remediation: Enable and configure cluster auditing in ACK to forward logs to Alibaba Cloud Log Service or a SIEM. Use Terraform to set up audit policies and ensure logs are stored securely off-cluster.

cis_alicloud_6.6.description: TDE with BYOK support provides increased transparency and control, increased security with an HSM-backed KMS service, and promotion of separation of duties. With TDE, data is encrypted at rest with a symmetric key (called the database encryption key). With BYOK support for TDE, the DEK can be protected with an asymmetric key that is stored in the KMS. Based on business needs or criticality of data, it is recommended that the TDE protector is encrypted by a key that is managed by the data owner.
cis_alicloud_6.6.remediation: Configure TDE with BYOK for RDS instances using Alibaba Cloud KMS to manage encryption keys. Use Terraform to associate customer-managed keys with database instances and enforce key rotation policies.

cis_alicloud_6.6.1.description: Pod Security Policy should be used to prevent privileged containers where possible and enforce namespace and workload configurations.
cis_alicloud_6.6.1.remediation: Enable and define Pod Security Policies in ACK clusters to restrict privileged containers and enforce security contexts. Use admission controllers to apply policies and audit compliance regularly.

cis_alicloud_6.6.2.description: Enable Cloud Security Center to provide a centralized view of security for your ACK clusters.
cis_alicloud_6.6.2.remediation: Activate Alibaba Cloud Security Center for ACK clusters via console or API. Configure security assessments and alerts to monitor cluster security posture and respond to threats.

cis_alicloud_6.6.3.description: Use ACK Sandboxed-Container to restrict untrusted workloads as an additional layer of protection when running in a multi-tenant environment.
cis_alicloud_6.6.3.remediation: Deploy ACK Sandboxed-Container for untrusted workloads using Alibaba Cloud console or Kubernetes manifests. Isolate workloads with gVisor or similar runtimes to enhance security boundaries.

cis_alicloud_6.6.4.description: ACK provides TEE-based confidential computing. This is a cloud-native and all-in-one solution based on Intel Software Guard Extensions (SGX). It aims to deliver and manage trusted applications and confidential computing applications by ensuring the security, integrity, and confidentiality of data in use.
cis_alicloud_6.6.4.remediation: Enable TEE-based confidential computing for ACK clusters via Alibaba Cloud console for sensitive workloads. Use SGX-enabled nodes and configure applications to leverage encrypted memory for data in use.

cis_alicloud_6.6.5.description: Use service account token volume projection to enhance security when you use service accounts.
cis_alicloud_6.6.5.remediation: Configure Kubernetes service account token volume projection in ACK clusters to issue short-lived tokens. Update pod specifications to use projected tokens and reduce the risk of token exposure.

cis_alicloud_6.7.remediation: Set the log_connections parameter to ON for Alibaba Cloud RDS PostgreSQL instances via parameter groups. Use Terraform to enforce this setting and monitor connection logs for security analysis.

cis_alicloud_6.8.remediation: Configure the log_disconnections parameter to ON for RDS PostgreSQL instances using Alibaba Cloud console or API. Review disconnection logs regularly to detect unauthorized access attempts.

cis_alicloud_6.9.remediation: Enable the log_duration parameter for RDS PostgreSQL to log query execution times. Adjust logging levels as needed and use Log Service to analyze performance and security issues.

cis_alicloud_7.1.description: Log Service is a complete real-time data logging service on Alibaba Cloud to support collection, shipping, search, storage and analysis for logs. It includes a user interface to call the Log Viewer and an API to management logs pragmatically. Log Service could automatically collect, process, and store your container and audit logs in a dedicated, persistent datastore. Container logs are collected from your containers. Audit logs are collected from the kube-apiserver or the deployed ingress.
cis_alicloud_7.1.remediation: Enable Log Service for ACK clusters via Alibaba Cloud console to collect and store logs. Configure log retention policies and use dashboards for monitoring and compliance reporting.

cis_alicloud_7.2.description: The monitoring service in Kubernetes Engine clusters depends on the Alibaba Cloud CloudMonitor agent to access additional system resources and application services in virtual machine instances. The monitor can access metrics about CPU utilization, some disk traffic metrics, network traffic, and disk IO information, which help to monitor signals and build operations in your Kubernetes Engine clusters.
cis_alicloud_7.2.remediation: Activate CloudMonitor for ACK clusters to collect metrics and monitor performance. Install the CloudMonitor agent on nodes and set up alerts for critical thresholds to ensure operational health.

cis_alicloud_7.3.description: In Kubernetes, authorizers interact by granting a permission if any authorizer grants the permission. The legacy authorizer in Kubernetes Engine grants broad, statically defined permissions. To ensure that RBAC limits permissions correctly, you must disable the legacy authorizer. RBAC has significant security advantages, can help you ensure that users only have access to specific cluster resources within their own namespace and is now stable in Kubernetes.
cis_alicloud_7.3.remediation: Enable RBAC and disable legacy authorization in ACK clusters via Kubernetes configuration. Define roles and role bindings to enforce least privilege access and regularly audit permissions.

cis_alicloud_7.4.description: Kubernetes Engine's cluster check feature helps you verify the system nodes and components healthy status. When you trigger the checking, the process would check on the health state of each node in your cluster and also the cluster configuration as kubelet\docker daemon\kernel and network iptables configuration, if there are fails consecutive health checks, the diagnose would report to admin for further repair.
cis_alicloud_7.4.remediation: Schedule weekly cluster health checks for ACK clusters using Alibaba Cloud console or automation scripts. Review check results and address any issues to maintain cluster reliability and security.

cis_alicloud_7.5.description: Dashboard is a web-based Kubernetes user interface. It can be used to deploy containerized applications to a Kubernetes cluster, troubleshoot your containerized application, and manage the cluster itself along with its attendant resources. You can use Dashboard to get an overview of applications running on your cluster, as well as for creating or modifying individual Kubernetes resources (such as Deployments, Jobs, DaemonSets, etc). For example, you can scale a Deployment, initiate a rolling update.
cis_alicloud_7.5.remediation: Disable the Kubernetes Dashboard in ACK clusters if not required, or restrict access using RBAC and network policies. Use kubectl or other secure management tools for cluster operations.

cis_alicloud_7.6.description: Basic authentication allows a user to authenticate to the cluster with a username and password and it is stored in plain text without any encryption. Disabling Basic authentication will prevent attacks like brute force. Its recommended to use either client certificate or RAM for authentication.
cis_alicloud_7.6.remediation: Disable basic authentication in ACK clusters and enable client certificate or RAM-based authentication. Update cluster configuration to use secure methods and regularly rotate credentials.

cis_alicloud_7.7.description: A network policy is a specification of how groups of pods are allowed to communicate with each other and other network endpoints. NetworkPolicy resources use labels to select pods and define rules which specify what traffic is allowed to the selected pods. The Kubernetes Network Policy API allows the cluster administrator to specify what pods are allowed to communicate with each other.
cis_alicloud_7.7.remediation: Enable Network Policy in ACK clusters using supported CNI plugins like Calico. Define policies to control pod-to-pod traffic and enforce segmentation for enhanced security.

cis_alicloud_7.8.description: Alibaba Cloud ENI (Elastic Network Interface) has supported assign ranges of internal IP addresses as aliases to a single virtual machine's ENI network interfaces. This is useful if you have lots of services running on a VM and you want to assign each service a different IP address without quota limitation.
cis_alicloud_7.8.remediation: Configure ENI multiple IP mode for ACK clusters using Terway network plugin. Assign multiple IPs to pods for scalability and manage network policies to control traffic flow.

cis_alicloud_7.9.description: A private cluster is a cluster that makes your master inaccessible from the public internet. In a private cluster, nodes do not have public IP addresses, so your workloads run in an environment that is isolated from the internet. Nodes have addresses only in the private address space. Nodes and masters communicate with each other privately using VPC peering.
cis_alicloud_7.9.remediation: Enable private cluster mode during Kubernetes cluster creation in Alibaba Cloud Container Service for Kubernetes (ACK). Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or Alibaba Cloud Resource Orchestration Service (ROS) to enforce this setting. Ensure VPC peering is configured for private communication between nodes and masters, and restrict public access via security groups and network ACLs.

cis_alicloud_8.1.description: The Advanced or Enterprise Edition enables threat detection for network and endpoints, providing malware detection, webshell detection and anomaly detection in Security Center.
cis_alicloud_8.1.remediation: Upgrade Security Center to Advanced or Enterprise Edition via the Alibaba Cloud console or API. Use IaC to define this configuration in templates for consistent deployment. Regularly audit subscription status and enable all threat detection features to enhance security monitoring and response capabilities.

cis_alicloud_8.2.remediation: Install and activate the Security Center agent on all ECS instances and other assets. Automate deployment using Alibaba Cloud Automation Orchestration Service (OOS) or scripts. Monitor agent status through Security Center dashboards and set up alerts for any unprotected endpoints to ensure comprehensive coverage.

cis_alicloud_8.3.description: Enable automatic quarantine in Security Center may 1ncure additional cost.
cis_alicloud_8.3.remediation: Enable automatic quarantine in Security Center settings to isolate compromised assets automatically. Configure quarantine policies based on risk levels and review cost implications. Use IaC to enforce this setting across environments and integrate with incident response workflows for swift action.

cis_alicloud_8.4.description: Enable webshell detection on all web servers to scans periodically the Web directories for detecting webshells on servers.
cis_alicloud_8.4.remediation: Activate webshell detection in Security Center for all web servers. Schedule regular scans and configure alerts for detections. Use IaC to apply this configuration uniformly and ensure web server directories are monitored for unauthorized scripts to prevent backdoor access.

cis_alicloud_8.5.description: Enable all risk item notification in Vulnerability, Baseline Risks, Alerts and Accesskey Leak event detection categories.
cis_alicloud_8.5.remediation: Configure notifications for high-risk items in Security Center, including vulnerabilities, baseline risks, alerts, and access key leaks. Set up email, SMS, or integration with messaging platforms like DingTalk. Use automation to ensure notification settings are applied across all accounts and review regularly.

cis_alicloud_8.6.description: Grant Security Center’s Cloud Platform Configuration Assessment the privilege to access other cloud product.
cis_alicloud_8.6.remediation: Assign necessary permissions to Security Center's Config Assessment feature using RAM roles and policies. Follow the principle of least privilege, granting only required access to cloud products. Use IaC to define these roles and audit permissions periodically to prevent over-provisioning.

cis_alicloud_8.7.description: Ensure that scheduled vulnerability scan is enabled on all servers.
cis_alicloud_8.7.remediation: Enable scheduled vulnerability scans in Security Center for all servers. Configure scan frequency (e.g., weekly) and scope. Use IaC to enforce this setting and integrate scan results with ticketing systems for remediation tracking. Regularly review and patch identified vulnerabilities.

cis_alicloud_8.8.description: The Enterprise Edition enables asset fingerprint collection for endpoints providing a collection of port, software, processes, scheduled tasks and middleware in Security Center.
cis_alicloud_8.8.remediation: Upgrade to Enterprise Edition and enable asset fingerprint collection. Configure automatic data refresh (e.g., every 30 days) via Security Center settings. Use IaC to apply this configuration and leverage collected data for asset management and security baselining to improve visibility.

cis_aws_1.1.description: Ensure contact email and telephone details for AWS accounts are current and map to more than one individual in your organization. An AWS account supports a number of contact details, and AWS will use these to contact the account owner if activity judged to be in breach of the Acceptable Use Policy or indicative of a likely security compromise is observed by the AWS Abuse team. Contact details should not be for a single individual, as circumstances may arise where that individual is unavailable.
cis_aws_1.1.remediation: Update contact details in AWS Account Settings to include multiple individuals' emails and phone numbers. Establish a process for regular reviews (e.g., quarterly) to ensure information remains current. Use AWS Organizations to manage contacts centrally and document procedures for handling AWS communications.

cis_aws_1.10.description: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of authentication assurance beyond traditional credentials. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in to the AWS Console, they will be prompted for their user name and password as well as for an authentication code from their physical or virtual MFA token. It is recommended that MFA be enabled for all accounts that have a console password.
cis_aws_1.10.remediation: Enable MFA for all IAM users with console access using virtual or hardware MFA devices. Use AWS IAM policies to enforce MFA for sensitive actions. Automate MFA enforcement with AWS Config rules or third-party tools, and regularly audit compliance to ensure no users are exempted.

cis_aws_1.11.description: AWS IAM users can access AWS resources using different types of credentials, such as passwords or access keys. It is recommended that all credentials that have been unused for 45 days or more be deactivated or removed.
cis_aws_1.11.remediation: Implement IAM credential lifecycle policies using AWS IAM Access Analyzer or custom scripts to identify unused credentials. Disable or delete passwords and access keys inactive for 45+ days. Use AWS Config or CloudTrail to monitor usage and automate remediation with Lambda functions for consistent enforcement.

cis_aws_1.12.description: AWS IAM users can access AWS resources using different types of credentials, such as passwords or access keys. It is recommended that all credentials that have been unused for 45 days or more be deactivated or removed.
cis_aws_1.12.remediation: Implement IAM credential lifecycle policies using AWS IAM Access Analyzer or custom scripts to identify unused credentials. Disable or delete passwords and access keys inactive for 45+ days. Use AWS Config or CloudTrail to monitor usage and automate remediation with Lambda functions for consistent enforcement.

cis_aws_1.13.description: Access keys are long-term credentials for an IAM user or the AWS account 'root' user. You can use access keys to sign programmatic requests to the AWS CLI or AWS API (directly or using the AWS SDK)
cis_aws_1.13.remediation: Ensure each IAM user has only one active access key at a time. Use IAM policies to restrict key creation and automate key rotation.
