cis_oci_3.2.2.remediation: Configure the --authorization-mode argument to a value other than AlwaysAllow, such as Webhook or RBAC. Use IaC to apply this setting, define granular authorization policies, and regularly test access controls to ensure proper enforcement.

cis_oci_3.2.3.remediation: Set the --client-ca-file argument to a valid CA certificate file for kubelet authentication. Use IaC to manage certificate deployment, enforce TLS encryption, and rotate certificates periodically to maintain secure authentication.

cis_oci_3.2.4.remediation: Configure the --read-only-port argument to 0 in kubelet settings. Automate this configuration with IaC, disable unnecessary ports across nodes, and implement network policies to restrict access to kubelet endpoints.

cis_oci_3.2.5.description: Do not disable timeouts on streaming connections.
cis_oci_3.2.5.remediation: Ensure the --streaming-connection-idle-timeout argument is not set to 0 in kubelet configuration. Use IaC to enforce appropriate timeout values, monitor connection durations, and apply security policies that prevent indefinite streaming sessions.

cis_oci_3.2.6.remediation: Configure the Kubelet with the --make-iptables-util-chains argument set to true in the kubelet configuration file or via command-line flags. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or Kubernetes manifests to enforce this setting across all nodes, ensuring consistent security posture and preventing manual misconfigurations.

cis_oci_3.2.7.description: Security relevant information should be captured. The --event-qps flag on the Kubelet can be used to limit the rate at which events are gathered. Setting this too low could result in relevant events not being logged, however the unlimited setting of 0 could result in a denial of service on the kubelet.
cis_oci_3.2.7.remediation: Set the --event-qps argument to a value that balances event capture with performance, such as 5 or 10, to avoid missing critical logs or causing denial of service. Implement this via Kubernetes configuration management tools like Kubeadm or Helm charts, and monitor logs to adjust as needed for your cluster's workload.

cis_oci_3.2.8.description: Setup TLS connection on the Kubelets.
cis_oci_3.2.8.remediation: Configure the Kubelet with --tls-cert-file and --tls-private-key-file arguments pointing to valid TLS certificates and keys. Use automated certificate management solutions like Cert-Manager in Kubernetes to generate and rotate certificates, and enforce this via RBAC policies to restrict access to certificate files, ensuring encrypted communication.

cis_oci_3.2.9.remediation: Ensure the --rotate-certificates argument is not set to false; it should be enabled or left default to allow automatic rotation of client certificates. Implement this in your kubelet configuration using IaC tools, and regularly audit certificate expiration to maintain secure authentication without manual intervention.

cis_oci_3.3.description: The Block Volume service provides the option to enable in-transit encryption for paravirtualized volume attachments on virtual machine (VM) instances.
cis_oci_3.3.remediation: Enable in-transit encryption for all Block Volume attachments on VM instances in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. Use Terraform or OCI CLI scripts to configure this during volume creation, and apply encryption policies via IAM to ensure all new volumes are encrypted, protecting data during transmission.

cis_oci_4.1.description: Using default tags is a way to ensure all resources that support tags are tagged during creation. Tags can be based on static or computed values. It is recommended to set up default tags early after root compartment creation to ensure all created resources will get tagged. Tags are scoped to Compartments and are inherited by Child Compartments. The recommendation is to create default tags like “CreatedBy” at the Root Compartment level to ensure all resources get tagged. When using Tags it is imp
cis_oci_4.1.remediation: Define default tags at the root compartment level in OCI, such as 'CreatedBy' and 'Environment', to automatically apply tags to all resources. Use OCI Policies or Terraform modules to enforce tagging compliance, and regularly review untagged resources via Cloud Guard or custom scripts to maintain governance.

cis_oci_4.1.1.description: The RBAC role cluster-admin provides wide-ranging powers over the environment and should be used only where and when needed.
cis_oci_4.1.1.remediation: Restrict the use of cluster-admin role to specific, trusted users or service accounts only. Implement least privilege access by creating custom RBAC roles with minimal permissions, and use Kubernetes admission controllers or OCI IAM policies to audit and enforce this, reducing the risk of excessive privileges.

cis_oci_4.1.5.description: The default service account should not be used to ensure that rights granted to applications can be more easily audited and reviewed.
cis_oci_4.1.5.remediation: Avoid using default service accounts; instead, create dedicated service accounts with specific permissions for each application. Use Kubernetes RBAC to assign roles, and enforce this via policy-as-code tools like OPA Gatekeeper, ensuring all service accounts are documented and regularly reviewed.

cis_oci_4.1.6.description: Service accounts tokens should not be mounted in pods except where the workload running in the pod explicitly needs to communicate with the API server
cis_oci_4.1.6.remediation: Configure pods to mount service account tokens only when necessary by setting automountServiceAccountToken to false in pod specifications. Use Kubernetes security contexts and audit pod configurations with tools like kube-bench to prevent unnecessary token exposure and reduce attack surface.

cis_oci_4.10.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when security lists are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.10.remediation: Create an Event Rule at the root compartment in OCI to monitor security list changes, and configure notifications via email, PagerDuty, or Slack. Use OCI CLI or Terraform to automate this setup, and ensure the rule is enabled across all child compartments for comprehensive visibility into network security modifications.

cis_oci_4.11.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when network security groups are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.11.remediation: Set up an Event Rule at the root compartment to track network security group changes, and link it to a notification topic. Automate this with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools, and regularly test the notification system to ensure alerts are received promptly for any security group modifications.

cis_oci_4.12.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when Network Gateways are created, updated, deleted, attached, detached, or moved. This recommendation includes Internet Gateways, Dynamic Routing Gateways, Service Gateways, Local Peering Gateways, and NAT Gateways. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.12.remediation: Configure an Event Rule at the root compartment to monitor all network gateway activities, and set up notifications via OCI services. Use automation scripts or Terraform to deploy this rule consistently, and review event logs periodically to detect unauthorized changes early.

cis_oci_4.13.description: VCN flow logs record details about traffic that has been accepted or rejected based on the security list rule.
cis_oci_4.13.remediation: Enable VCN flow logging for all subnets in your OCI tenancy to capture network traffic details. Use OCI Console, CLI, or Terraform to configure flow logs, and store logs in Object Storage with encryption enabled for analysis and auditing of security list rule effectiveness.

cis_oci_4.14.description: Cloud Guard detects misconfigured resources and insecure activity within a tenancy and provides security administrators with the visibility to resolve these issues. Upon detection, Cloud Guard can suggest, assist, or take corrective actions to mitigate these issues. Cloud Guard should be enabled in the root compartment of your tenancy with the default configuration, activity detectors and responders.
cis_oci_4.14.remediation: Enable Cloud Guard in the root compartment of your OCI tenancy with default detectors and responders. Use OCI Policies to enforce this across all compartments, and integrate with Event Rules for automated notifications and remediation actions to maintain continuous security monitoring.

cis_oci_4.15.description: Cloud Guard detects misconfigured resources and insecure activity within a tenancy and provides security administrators with the visibility to resolve these issues. Upon detection, Cloud Guard generates a Problem. It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when Oracle Cloud Guard Problems are created, dismissed or remediated. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments. It is recommended to create the Event rule at the root c
cis_oci_4.15.remediation: Create an Event Rule at the root compartment to monitor Cloud Guard problem events, and configure notifications to alert security teams. Automate this setup with IaC tools, and ensure responders are defined to take corrective actions, enhancing incident response capabilities for detected issues.

cis_oci_4.16.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Vault securely stores master encryption keys that protect your encrypted data. You can use the Vault service to rotate keys to generate new cryptographic material. Periodically rotating keys limits the amount of data encrypted by one key version.
cis_oci_4.16.remediation: Enable automatic key rotation for Customer Managed Keys (CMKs) in OCI Vault, setting a rotation schedule of at least annually. Use OCI Policies to enforce rotation, and monitor key usage with auditing tools to ensure compliance, reducing the risk of key compromise over time.

cis_oci_4.17.description: Object Storage write logs will log all write requests made to objects in a bucket.
cis_oci_4.17.remediation: Enable write-level logging for all Object Storage buckets in OCI to capture write requests. Configure this via the OCI Console or CLI, and store logs in a separate bucket with encryption for security analysis. Use IAM policies to restrict access to logs and regularly review them for suspicious activity.

cis_oci_4.18.description: It is recommended that an Event Rule and Notification be set up when a user in the via OCI local authentication. Event Rules are compartment-scoped and will detect events in child compartments. This Event rule is required to be created at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.18.remediation: Set up an Event Rule at the root compartment to monitor local OCI user authentication events, and configure notifications for real-time alerts. Use automation tools to deploy this rule, and integrate with SIEM systems for centralized logging and analysis of authentication attempts.

cis_oci_4.3.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when Identity Providers are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments. It is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.3.remediation: Create an Event Rule at the root compartment to track Identity Provider changes, and link it to a notification topic. Automate this configuration with Terraform or OCI CLI, and ensure the rule is active to detect any modifications that could impact authentication security.

cis_oci_4.3.1.description: There are a variety of CNI plugins available for Kubernetes. If the CNI in use does not support Network Policies it may not be possible to effectively restrict traffic in the cluster.
cis_oci_4.3.1.remediation: Ensure the latest version of a CNI plugin that supports Network Policies, such as Calico or Cilium, is used in your Kubernetes cluster. Update CNI via cluster management tools or IaC, and test network policy enforcement to maintain effective traffic isolation and security.

cis_oci_4.3.2.description: Use network policies to isolate traffic in your cluster network.
cis_oci_4.3.2.remediation: Define and apply Network Policies for all namespaces in your Kubernetes cluster to control pod-to-pod communication. Use tools like Helm or Kustomize to manage policies as code, and regularly audit with kube-score or similar to ensure no namespace is left without policies, reducing lateral movement risks.

cis_oci_4.4.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when Identity Provider Group Mappings are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments. It is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.4.remediation: Configure an Event Rule at the root compartment to monitor IdP group mapping changes, and set up notifications for alerts. Use automation scripts to enforce this rule, and review mapping logs periodically to ensure group assignments align with least privilege principles.

cis_oci_4.5.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when IAM Groups are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.5.remediation: Set up an Event Rule at the root compartment to track IAM group modifications, and configure notifications via OCI. Automate this with Terraform, and integrate with change management processes to review group changes for security compliance and unauthorized access.

cis_oci_4.6.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when IAM Policies are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.6.remediation: Create an Event Rule at the root compartment to monitor IAM policy changes, and enable notifications for immediate alerts. Use IaC to manage policies and ensure changes are logged and reviewed, applying RBAC to restrict policy modifications to authorized personnel only.

cis_oci_4.7.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when IAM Users are created, updated, deleted, capabilities updated, or state updated. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.7.remediation: Configure an Event Rule at the root compartment to track IAM user changes, and set up notifications for security teams. Automate this with OCI CLI or Terraform, and regularly audit user activities and permissions to detect anomalies and enforce least privilege access controls.

cis_oci_4.8.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when Virtual Cloud Networks are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.8.remediation: Create an Event Rule at the root compartment level in OCI to monitor VCN changes, and configure a Notification topic (e.g., via OCI Notifications or third-party integrations) to alert administrators. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform to automate this setup and ensure it's applied consistently across environments.

cis_oci_4.9.description: It is recommended to setup an Event Rule and Notification that gets triggered when route tables are created, updated or deleted. Event Rules are compartment scoped and will detect events in child compartments, it is recommended to create the Event rule at the root compartment level.
cis_oci_4.9.remediation: Set up an Event Rule at the root compartment in OCI to detect route table modifications, and link it to a Notification service for real-time alerts. Implement this using IaC templates to enforce the configuration and integrate with monitoring dashboards for visibility.

cis_oci_5.1.1.description: A bucket is a logical container for storing objects. It is associated with a single compartment that has policies that determine what action a user can perform on a bucket and on all the objects in the bucket. By Default a newly created bucket is private. It is recommended that no bucket be publicly accessible.
cis_oci_5.1.1.remediation: Ensure all Object Storage buckets in OCI have public access blocked by setting bucket policies to restrict access to authorized users only. Use IaC to define bucket configurations with 'publicAccessType' set to 'NoPublicAccess' and regularly audit buckets using CSPM tools to detect and remediate any public exposures.

cis_oci_5.1.2.description: Oracle Object Storage buckets support encryption with a Customer Managed Key (CMK). By default, Object Storage buckets are encrypted with an Oracle managed key.
cis_oci_5.1.2.remediation: Encrypt Object Storage buckets with Customer Managed Keys (CMK) using OCI Key Management service. Configure bucket encryption settings via the OCI console, CLI, or IaC (e.g., Terraform) to specify a CMK, and ensure key rotation policies are in place to maintain security compliance.

cis_oci_5.1.3.description: A bucket is a logical container for storing objects. Object versioning is enabled at the bucket level and is disabled by default upon creation. Versioning directs Object Storage to automatically create an object version each time a new object is uploaded, an existing object is overwritten, or when an object is deleted. You can enable object versioning at bucket creation time or later.
cis_oci_5.1.3.remediation: Enable versioning on all Object Storage buckets in OCI to protect against accidental deletions or overwrites. Use IaC to set 'versioning' to 'Enabled' during bucket creation, and implement lifecycle policies to manage older versions and control storage costs effectively.

cis_oci_5.2.1.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Block Volume service lets you dynamically provision and manage block storage volumes. By default, the Oracle service manages the keys that encrypt block volumes. Block Volumes can also be encrypted using a customer managed key. Terminated Block Volumes cannot be recovered and any data on a terminated volume is permanently lost. However, Block Volumes can exist in a terminated state within the OCI Portal and CLI for some time after deleting. As such, any Block Volumes
cis_oci_5.2.1.remediation: Encrypt Block Volumes with Customer Managed Keys (CMK) using OCI Key Management. Specify encryption settings when creating volumes via the OCI console, CLI, or IaC, and ensure proper key access policies are configured to restrict unauthorized usage.

cis_oci_5.2.2.description: When you launch a virtual machine (VM) or bare metal instance based on a platform image or custom image, a new boot volume for the instance is created in the same compartment. That boot volume is associated with that instance until you terminate the instance. By default, the Oracle service manages the keys that encrypt this boot volume. Boot Volumes can also be encrypted using a customer managed key.
cis_oci_5.2.2.remediation: Encrypt boot volumes with Customer Managed Keys (CMK) in OCI by configuring encryption settings during instance launch or volume creation. Use IaC to enforce CMK encryption for all boot volumes and regularly audit configurations to ensure compliance with encryption policies.

cis_oci_5.3.1.description: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure File Storage service (FSS) provides a durable, scalable, secure, enterprise-grade network file system. By default, the Oracle service manages the keys that encrypt FSS file systems. FSS file systems can also be encrypted using a customer managed key.
cis_oci_5.3.1.remediation: Encrypt File Storage Systems with Customer Managed Keys (CMK) using OCI Key Management. Set encryption parameters when creating or updating file systems via the OCI console, CLI, or IaC, and implement key rotation schedules to enhance data security.

cis_oci_5.4.2.description: Disable access to the Kubernetes API from outside the node network if it is not required.
cis_oci_5.4.2.remediation: Create OKE clusters with Private Endpoint enabled and Public Access disabled to restrict Kubernetes API exposure. Use IaC (e.g., Terraform) to define cluster configurations with 'endpointConfig' set to private, and apply network security groups to limit access to authorized IP ranges.

cis_oci_5.4.3.description: Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses. Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses.
cis_oci_5.4.3.remediation: Configure OKE clusters to use Private Nodes by setting node pool configurations to assign only private IPs. Use IaC to enforce this during cluster creation and update existing clusters via the OCI console or CLI to remove public IP assignments.

cis_oci_5.4.4.description: Use Network Policy to restrict pod to pod traffic within a cluster and segregate workloads.
cis_oci_5.4.4.remediation: Enable Network Policy in OKE clusters and configure appropriate policies to control pod-to-pod communication. Use Kubernetes manifests or IaC tools to define and apply network policies, and regularly review them to align with least-privilege access principles.

cis_oci_6.2.description: When you create a cloud resource such as an instance, block volume, or cloud network, you must specify to which compartment you want the resource to belong. Placing resources in the root compartment makes it difficult to organize and isolate those resources.
cis_oci_6.2.remediation: Avoid creating resources in the root compartment by enforcing compartment policies and using IaC to specify target compartments for all resources. Regularly audit compartments with CSPM tools to identify and move any root compartment resources to appropriate sub-compartments.

cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_1.description: Implement a backup solution that automatically and continuously backs up critical data to ensure data recovery in case of loss or corruption.
cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_1.remediation: Configure automated backup solutions for critical data using cloud-native services like OCI Database backups or third-party tools. Use IaC to define backup policies with retention schedules and test restoration procedures regularly to verify effectiveness.

cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_2.description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts to enhance security against unauthorized access.
cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_2.remediation: Enable MFA for all users in IAM settings using hardware or software tokens. Enforce MFA via identity policies and use IaC to automate MFA configuration, ensuring it's applied consistently across all cloud accounts and services.

cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_3.description: Enable automatic updates and apply patches quickly to protect systems from known vulnerabilities.
cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_3.remediation: Configure automatic patch management for instances and services using cloud-native tools like OCI OS Management. Use IaC to set up patch policies and schedule regular compliance checks to ensure systems are up-to-date.

cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_4.description: Implement secure configurations for hardware and software to reduce attack surfaces and misconfigurations.
cisa_ce_v1_Booting_Up_4.remediation: Apply secure configuration baselines using IaC templates and compliance tools. Regularly audit configurations against benchmarks like CIS, and use CSPM platforms to detect and remediate deviations automatically.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Crisis_Response_1.description: Develop and lead the implementation of an incident response and disaster recovery plan to handle security crises effectively.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Crisis_Response_1.remediation: Create and document an incident response plan with defined roles, procedures, and communication channels. Conduct regular tabletop exercises and update the plan based on lessons learned to ensure readiness for real incidents.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Crisis_Response_2.description: Leverage business impact assessments to prioritize recovery efforts and resource allocation during disruptions.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Crisis_Response_2.remediation: Perform business impact assessments to identify critical systems and data. Use the results to inform disaster recovery strategies and update plans periodically to reflect changes in business operations.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_1.description: Monitor network activity to detect anomalies and potential security threats in real-time.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_1.remediation: Enable logging and monitoring services like OCI Audit and Cloud Guard. Use IaC to configure log collection and set up alerts for suspicious activities, integrating with SIEM tools for comprehensive visibility.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_2.description: Understand and implement data protection measures such as encryption to safeguard sensitive information.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_2.remediation: Encrypt data at rest and in transit using cloud-native encryption services. Use IaC to enforce encryption policies for storage and databases, and regularly audit configurations to ensure compliance.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_3.description: Utilize malware protection capabilities to detect and prevent malicious software infections.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_3.remediation: Enable threat detection services like OCI Cloud Guard and integrate with antivirus solutions. Use IaC to deploy and configure malware protection across instances, and set up automated scans and alerts.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_4.description: Establish regular automated backups to ensure data availability and recovery in case of incidents.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Data_4.remediation: Set up automated backup schedules for critical data using services like OCI Backup. Use IaC to define backup policies with appropriate retention periods and test restoration processes regularly.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_1.description: Foster a culture of cybersecurity awareness to encourage employees to adopt secure online practices.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_1.remediation: Implement regular security awareness training programs and phishing simulations. Develop policies and procedures to promote secure behavior and provide resources for employees to report security concerns.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_2.description: Educate staff about risks like phishing and business email compromise to reduce social engineering attacks.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_2.remediation: Conduct targeted training sessions on phishing and email security. Use simulated phishing campaigns to test employee readiness and update training materials based on emerging threats.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_3.description: Maintain awareness of current cybersecurity events and trends to adapt security measures proactively.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_3.remediation: Subscribe to threat intelligence feeds and participate in security communities. Regularly review and update security policies based on current events and conduct briefings to keep staff informed.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_4.description: Ensure all staff receive basic cybersecurity training to understand common threats and safe practices.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_4.remediation: Develop and implement a mandatory cybersecurity training program for all employees. Regularly update training materials to cover emerging threats and conduct periodic assessments to verify understanding.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_5.description: Identify and document available cybersecurity training resources for staff to access.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Staff_5.remediation: Create an inventory of internal and external training resources, such as online courses or workshops. Communicate this list to employees and ensure it is regularly reviewed and updated.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_1.description: Require multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all user accounts to add an extra layer of security beyond passwords.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_1.remediation: Enable MFA policies in cloud IAM services (e.g., AWS IAM, Azure AD, GCP Cloud Identity) for all users. Use infrastructure as code (IaC) tools like Terraform to enforce MFA settings and regularly audit compliance.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_2.description: Grant access based on need-to-know and least privilege principles to minimize unnecessary permissions.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_2.remediation: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) in cloud environments, assigning minimal permissions required for tasks. Use policy-as-code tools to define and enforce access policies, and conduct regular reviews of IAM roles and permissions.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_3.description: Enforce the use of unique passwords for all user accounts to prevent credential reuse and attacks.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_3.remediation: Configure password policies in cloud identity services to require complexity, expiration, and uniqueness. Use automated checks to monitor compliance and integrate with identity providers to enforce these rules across systems.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_4.description: Develop IT policies to manage changes in user status, such as onboarding, offboarding, or role changes.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Surroundings_4.remediation: Automate user lifecycle management using tools like AWS IAM Access Analyzer or Azure AD Identity Governance. Implement procedures to revoke access promptly upon status changes and use IaC to enforce policy configurations.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_1.description: Maintain inventories of hardware and software assets to understand what is on your network.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_1.remediation: Use cloud asset management tools (e.g., AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory) to automatically discover and track resources. Implement tagging strategies and regular scans to keep inventories up-to-date.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_2.description: Maintain inventories of network connections to monitor who is accessing your network.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_2.remediation: Deploy network monitoring tools (e.g., AWS VPC Flow Logs, Azure Network Watcher, GCP VPC Flow Logs) to log and analyze connections. Use automated alerts for unusual activity and enforce security groups or firewall rules to restrict access.

cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_3.description: Identify and classify information residing on your network to protect sensitive data.
cisa_ce_v1_Your_Systems_3.remediation: Use data discovery and classification tools (e.g., AWS Macie, Azure Information Protection, GCP Data Loss Prevention) to scan storage resources. Apply encryption and access controls based on data sensitivity, and implement policies to prevent public exposure.

fedramp_moderate_AC_1.description: Establish policies and procedures for access control to guide security practices.
fedramp_moderate_AC_1.remediation: Document access control policies in organizational security manuals. Regularly review and update these documents, and ensure staff are trained on procedures through governance processes.

fedramp_moderate_AC_10.description: Control concurrent sessions to prevent unauthorized access and resource exhaustion.
fedramp_moderate_AC_10.remediation: Configure session limits in identity providers (e.g., Azure AD, AWS IAM) and applications. Monitor session activity with logging tools and enforce policies through manual audits and procedural reviews.

fedramp_moderate_AC_11.description: Implement device lock mechanisms to secure unattended systems.
fedramp_moderate_AC_11.remediation: Enforce device lock policies on endpoints using management tools (e.g., MDM solutions). Train users on locking procedures and conduct periodic checks to ensure compliance through manual oversight.

fedramp_moderate_AC_11_1.description: Use pattern-hiding displays to prevent shoulder surfing and unauthorized viewing.
fedramp_moderate_AC_11_1.remediation: Configure display settings on devices to hide sensitive information when not in active use. Implement screen timeout policies and educate users on secure display practices through organizational guidelines.

fedramp_moderate_AC_12.description: Terminate user sessions after periods of inactivity or specific events to reduce risk.
fedramp_moderate_AC_12.remediation: Set session timeout policies in cloud services and applications. Use automated tools to enforce these settings and conduct regular audits to verify that sessions are properly terminated as per procedures.

fedramp_moderate_AC_14.description: Define permitted actions that do not require identification or authentication, limiting them to low-risk activities.
fedramp_moderate_AC_14.remediation: Document and restrict anonymous access in system configurations. Use access control lists and policies to allow only necessary unauthenticated actions, and review these settings periodically through manual processes.

fedramp_moderate_AC_17.description: Secure remote access to systems to prevent unauthorized entry and data breaches.
fedramp_moderate_AC_17.remediation: Implement VPNs, bastion hosts, or zero-trust networks for remote access. Use encryption (e.g., TLS) and multi-factor authentication, and enforce policies through IaC to ensure configurations are consistent and secure.

fedramp_moderate_AC_17_1.description: Monitor and control remote access sessions to detect and respond to anomalies.
fedramp_moderate_AC_17_1.remediation: Deploy logging and monitoring tools (e.g., AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor) to track remote access. Set up alerts for suspicious activities and use automated scripts to enforce session controls and review logs regularly.

fedramp_moderate_AC_17_2.description: Protect confidentiality and integrity of remote access using encryption.
fedramp_moderate_AC_17_2.remediation: Enforce encryption for all remote connections, such as using TLS for web access and IPsec for VPNs. Configure cloud services to require encrypted protocols and use policy-as-code to maintain these settings across environments.

fedramp_moderate_AC_17_3.description: Manage access control points for remote connections to centralize and secure entry points.
fedramp_moderate_AC_17_3.remediation: Use managed services like AWS Direct Connect or Azure ExpressRoute for controlled access. Implement network security groups and firewalls to restrict traffic, and automate configuration checks to ensure only authorized points are active.

fedramp_moderate_AC_17_4.description: Control privileged commands and access during remote sessions to prevent misuse.
fedramp_moderate_AC_17_4.remediation: Implement just-in-time access and privilege escalation controls (e.g., AWS IAM Roles, Azure PIM). Use session recording and monitoring tools, and enforce policies through automated audits to limit privileged actions to authorized users.

fedramp_moderate_AC_18.description: Secure wireless access to networks to prevent unauthorized connectivity.
fedramp_moderate_AC_18.remediation: Develop and enforce wireless security policies, including the use of WPA3 encryption. Conduct regular scans for rogue access points and train staff on secure wireless practices through manual oversight and documentation.

fedramp_moderate_AC_18_1.description: Require authentication and encryption for wireless networks to protect data in transit.
fedramp_moderate_AC_18_1.remediation: Configure wireless networks to use strong authentication (e.g., 802.1X) and encryption protocols. Regularly update firmware and review configurations through procedural checks to ensure compliance with security standards.

fedramp_moderate_AC_18_3.description: Disable wireless networking when not required to reduce attack surface.
fedramp_moderate_AC_18_3.remediation: Establish policies to turn off wireless interfaces on devices when not in use. Use device management tools to enforce this and conduct periodic audits to verify compliance through manual reviews.

fedramp_moderate_AC_18_4.description: Restrict users from configuring wireless settings to maintain security controls.
fedramp_moderate_AC_18_4.remediation: Apply group policies or MDM settings to limit user permissions for wireless configuration. Educate users on approved procedures and perform regular checks to ensure settings are not altered without authorization.

fedramp_moderate_AC_18_5.description: Control antennas and transmission power levels to limit wireless signal range and exposure.
fedramp_moderate_AC_18_5.remediation: Configure wireless equipment to use minimal necessary transmission power. Document and review these settings periodically, and train personnel on proper deployment through organizational guidelines.

fedramp_moderate_AC_19.description: This control requires establishing and enforcing access control policies for mobile devices that connect to organizational systems, including authentication and authorization mechanisms.
fedramp_moderate_AC_19.remediation: Develop and document organizational policies for mobile device access, including procedures for device registration, authentication requirements, and remote wipe capabilities. Implement mobile device management (MDM) solutions to enforce these policies and conduct regular training for users on secure mobile device usage.

fedramp_moderate_AC_19_5.description: This control mandates the use of full device or container-based encryption on mobile devices to protect sensitive data stored or processed on these devices.
fedramp_moderate_AC_19_5.remediation: Define and enforce encryption requirements for mobile devices in organizational policies, specifying full device encryption or container-based solutions. Use MDM tools to configure and monitor encryption settings, and ensure users are trained on enabling and maintaining encryption on their devices.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2.description: This control involves managing user accounts, including creation, modification, monitoring, and deletion, to ensure proper access control and reduce security risks.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2.remediation: Implement automated account management processes using IAM tools to enforce least privilege, regular reviews, and timely deprovisioning. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to define account policies and integrate with HR systems for automated lifecycle management.

fedramp_moderate_AC_20.description: This control requires establishing policies and procedures for the use of external systems, such as third-party services or devices, to protect organizational information.
fedramp_moderate_AC_20.remediation: Create and document policies that define authorized use cases, security requirements, and risk assessments for external systems. Conduct regular audits and user training to ensure compliance, and implement technical controls like network segmentation where applicable.

fedramp_moderate_AC_20_1.description: This control specifies limits on the authorized use of external systems to prevent unauthorized access or data exposure.
fedramp_moderate_AC_20_1.remediation: Define clear usage boundaries in organizational policies, such as restricting data transfers or access levels. Enforce these limits through technical measures like access controls and monitoring, and review external system agreements regularly for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_AC_20_2.description: This control restricts the use of portable storage devices to mitigate risks of data loss, malware introduction, or unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_AC_20_2.remediation: Establish policies that prohibit or tightly control the use of portable storage devices, including encryption and approval requirements. Use endpoint protection tools to block unauthorized devices and conduct awareness training for users on the risks involved.

fedramp_moderate_AC_21.description: This control involves establishing procedures for securely sharing information with external entities, ensuring data integrity and confidentiality.
fedramp_moderate_AC_21.remediation: Develop and document information sharing protocols that include encryption, access controls, and data classification. Use secure communication channels and conduct regular reviews of sharing practices to align with organizational security policies.

fedramp_moderate_AC_22.description: This control requires managing publicly accessible content to prevent unauthorized disclosure of sensitive information.
fedramp_moderate_AC_22.remediation: Implement strict access controls and regular audits for public-facing resources like websites or storage buckets. Use web application firewalls and content scanning tools to detect and remediate exposures, and enforce policies for content review before publication.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_1.description: This control mandates automated management of system accounts to reduce manual errors and ensure consistent enforcement of account policies.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_1.remediation: Deploy automated tools for account provisioning, modification, and deprovisioning using IAM services. Integrate with identity providers and use IaC to define account configurations, ensuring regular audits and compliance with least privilege principles.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_11.description: This control specifies conditions for account usage, such as time-based or location-based restrictions, to enhance security.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_11.remediation: Configure IAM policies to enforce usage conditions like session timeouts or IP whitelisting. Use conditional access policies in cloud environments and monitor for violations through logging and alerting mechanisms.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_12.description: This control requires monitoring accounts for atypical usage patterns to detect potential security incidents or unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_12.remediation: Implement user behavior analytics (UBA) and SIEM tools to track login attempts, access patterns, and anomalies. Set up automated alerts for suspicious activities and conduct regular reviews of audit logs to identify and respond to deviations.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_13.description: This control involves disabling accounts for high-risk individuals, such as those leaving the organization or under investigation, to prevent unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_13.remediation: Establish procedures for timely account deactivation based on HR events or security alerts. Use automated workflows to revoke access and credentials, and ensure integration between IAM systems and HR databases for prompt action.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_2.description: This control mandates automated management of temporary and emergency accounts to limit their lifespan and access scope.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_2.remediation: Use IAM tools to create temporary accounts with expiration dates and restricted permissions. Implement approval workflows for emergency access and automate deprovisioning after use, with logging to track all activities.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_3.description: This control requires disabling accounts that are no longer needed, such as those for inactive users or terminated employees, to reduce attack surface.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_3.remediation: Automate account disablement based on inactivity thresholds or HR triggers using IAM policies. Conduct regular access reviews to identify and deactivate unused accounts, and ensure logging is in place for audit trails.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_4.description: This control involves automating audit actions for account management activities to ensure accountability and compliance.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_4.remediation: Enable comprehensive logging for all account-related events in IAM systems. Use SIEM tools to aggregate and analyze logs, and set up automated reports and alerts for critical actions like privilege changes or failed logins.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_5.description: This control mandates implementing inactivity logout mechanisms to terminate sessions after a period of inactivity, reducing the risk of unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_5.remediation: Configure session timeouts in IAM policies and application settings. Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) to enhance security and monitor for stale sessions with automated tools to enforce logout policies.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_7.description: This control requires managing privileged user accounts with enhanced security measures, such as stricter access controls and monitoring.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_7.remediation: Implement privileged access management (PAM) solutions to enforce just-in-time access and session recording. Use role-based access control (RBAC) to limit privileges and conduct regular audits of privileged account activities.

fedramp_moderate_AC_2_9.description: This control imposes restrictions on the use of shared and group accounts to ensure individual accountability and reduce security risks.
fedramp_moderate_AC_2_9.remediation: Minimize the use of shared accounts by assigning unique credentials where possible. For necessary shared accounts, enforce strong authentication, logging, and regular review of access permissions to maintain control.

fedramp_moderate_AC_3.description: This control involves enforcing access control policies to ensure that only authorized users can access organizational systems and data.
fedramp_moderate_AC_3.remediation: Implement robust access enforcement mechanisms using IAM policies, network ACLs, and application-level controls. Regularly review and update access policies to align with least privilege, and use automated tools to detect and remediate misconfigurations.

fedramp_moderate_AC_4.description: This control requires enforcing information flow policies to control how data moves within and between systems, preventing unauthorized disclosure.
fedramp_moderate_AC_4.remediation: Define and implement information flow policies using network segmentation, firewalls, and data loss prevention (DLP) tools. Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance and update policies based on changing data classification and risk assessments.

fedramp_moderate_AC_4_21.description: This control mandates the physical or logical separation of information flows to isolate sensitive data and reduce the risk of cross-contamination.
fedramp_moderate_AC_4_21.remediation: Use network segmentation, VLANs, or cloud security groups to separate information flows. Implement encryption for data in transit and conduct regular reviews to ensure separation is maintained as systems evolve.

fedramp_moderate_AC_4_4.description: This control involves enforcing flow control for encrypted information to ensure that only authorized entities can decrypt and access the data.
fedramp_moderate_AC_4_4.remediation: Implement encryption key management and access controls to restrict decryption to authorized users or systems. Use secure protocols for data transmission and regularly audit encryption configurations to prevent unauthorized access.

fedramp_moderate_AC_5.description: This control requires separating duties among individuals to prevent fraud, error, or misuse of system privileges.
fedramp_moderate_AC_5.remediation: Define and enforce separation of duties (SoD) policies in IAM systems using RBAC. Conduct regular access reviews to detect conflicts and use automated tools to alert on violations, ensuring critical functions are distributed appropriately.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6.description: This control mandates applying the principle of least privilege, ensuring users and systems have only the minimum permissions necessary to perform their functions.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6.remediation: Implement least privilege by defining granular IAM roles and policies. Use automated tools to scan for excessive permissions and conduct regular access reviews. Enforce just-in-time access for privileged tasks to minimize standing privileges.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_1.description: This control involves authorizing access to security functions, such as audit or configuration management, to trusted personnel only.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_1.remediation: Establish strict access controls for security functions using RBAC and multi-factor authentication. Document authorization procedures and conduct regular audits to ensure only authorized individuals can modify security settings or access sensitive logs.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_10.description: This control requires that non-privileged users are prevented from executing privileged functions to enforce least privilege principles.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_10.remediation: Establish and enforce policies that restrict privileged function execution to authorized roles only. Regularly review user permissions and implement role-based access controls (RBAC) to ensure compliance.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_2.description: This control mandates that non-privileged access is used for non-security functions to minimize risk exposure.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_2.remediation: Define and assign minimal necessary permissions for non-security tasks using RBAC. Conduct periodic audits to verify that users do not have excessive privileges.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_3.description: This control restricts network access to privileged commands to prevent unauthorized remote execution.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_3.remediation: Implement network segmentation and access controls to limit privileged command access to trusted sources. Use firewalls and VPNs to secure administrative connections.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_5.description: This control focuses on managing privileged accounts to prevent misuse and ensure accountability.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_5.remediation: Create and enforce policies for privileged account usage, including strong authentication and regular reviews. Use just-in-time access and monitor account activities.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_7.description: This control requires periodic review of user privileges to maintain least privilege and detect anomalies.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_7.remediation: Schedule regular audits of user permissions and adjust as needed. Implement automated tools to flag excessive privileges and enforce recertification processes.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_8.description: This control ensures that code execution occurs at appropriate privilege levels to reduce security risks.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_8.remediation: Configure systems to run applications with minimal necessary privileges. Use security policies and sandboxing to isolate high-risk code executions.

fedramp_moderate_AC_6_9.description: This control mandates logging of privileged function usage to enable monitoring and auditing.
fedramp_moderate_AC_6_9.remediation: Enable audit logging for all privileged actions and centralize logs for analysis. Set up alerts for suspicious activities and regularly review logs for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_AC_7.description: This control limits unsuccessful logon attempts to prevent brute-force attacks and unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_AC_7.remediation: Configure account lockout policies after a set number of failed attempts. Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) and monitor logon events for anomalies.

fedramp_moderate_AC_8.description: This control requires displaying system use notifications to inform users of acceptable use policies.
fedramp_moderate_AC_8.remediation: Implement banners or notifications at logon points that outline usage terms. Regularly update and test these notifications to ensure visibility and compliance.

fedramp_moderate_AT_1.description: This control involves establishing and maintaining policies and procedures for security training and awareness.
fedramp_moderate_AT_1.remediation: Develop and document comprehensive training policies, assign responsibilities, and review them annually. Ensure procedures are communicated and accessible to all personnel.

fedramp_moderate_AT_2.description: This control ensures personnel receive literacy training and awareness on security practices.
fedramp_moderate_AT_2.remediation: Provide regular security awareness training covering threats and best practices. Use engaging formats and track participation to maintain effectiveness.

fedramp_moderate_AT_2_2.description: This control focuses on training personnel to recognize and respond to insider threats.
fedramp_moderate_AT_2_2.remediation: Include insider threat scenarios in training programs and teach reporting procedures. Foster a culture of security and conduct periodic drills.

fedramp_moderate_AT_2_3.description: This control addresses training on social engineering and data mining techniques to enhance defense.
fedramp_moderate_AT_2_3.remediation: Educate staff on common social engineering tactics and safe data handling. Simulate phishing attacks and provide feedback to improve resilience.

fedramp_moderate_AT_3.description: This control requires role-based training tailored to specific job functions and security responsibilities.
fedramp_moderate_AT_3.remediation: Develop training modules for different roles and ensure completion as part of onboarding. Update content regularly to reflect evolving threats and technologies.

fedramp_moderate_AT_4.description: This control mandates maintaining records of security training activities for accountability.
fedramp_moderate_AT_4.remediation: Use a centralized system to track training completion and certifications. Retain records as per policy and audit them periodically for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_AU_1.description: This control involves creating and managing policies and procedures for audit and accountability.
fedramp_moderate_AU_1.remediation: Document audit policies outlining roles, responsibilities, and processes. Review and update procedures regularly to align with organizational changes.

fedramp_moderate_AU_10.description: This control ensures non-repudiation by preventing users from denying their actions through audit mechanisms.
fedramp_moderate_AU_10.remediation: Implement digital signatures, timestamps, and secure logging to verify actions. Use cryptographic methods and maintain chain of custody for audit trails.

fedramp_moderate_AU_11.description: This control specifies retention periods for audit records to support investigations and compliance.
fedramp_moderate_AU_11.remediation: Define and enforce retention policies based on regulatory requirements. Use secure storage solutions and regularly test retrieval processes.

fedramp_moderate_AU_12.description: This control requires generating audit records for system events to enable monitoring and analysis.
fedramp_moderate_AU_12.remediation: Enable logging across all critical systems and services. Use tools like AWS CloudTrail or Azure Monitor to automate log generation and ensure comprehensive coverage.

fedramp_moderate_AU_12_1.description: This control mandates a system-wide, time-correlated audit trail for cohesive event analysis.
fedramp_moderate_AU_12_1.remediation: Implement centralized logging with synchronized timestamps across systems. Use SIEM solutions to correlate events and detect anomalies in real-time.

fedramp_moderate_AU_12_3.description: This control ensures audit records capture changes made by authorized individuals for accountability.
fedramp_moderate_AU_12_3.remediation: Configure logging to include user identities and actions for all modifications. Review logs regularly to verify authorized changes and detect unauthorized activities.

fedramp_moderate_AU_2.description: This control defines the types of events that must be logged to support security monitoring.
fedramp_moderate_AU_2.remediation: Identify and enable logging for critical events such as logins and data access. Use cloud-native services to automate event logging and ensure consistency.

fedramp_moderate_AU_3.description: This control specifies the content that must be included in audit records for completeness.
fedramp_moderate_AU_3.remediation: Ensure audit logs contain essential details like timestamps, user IDs, and actions. Configure systems to capture required fields and validate log content periodically.

fedramp_moderate_AU_3_1.description: This control requires additional audit information to enhance the detail and usefulness of logs.
fedramp_moderate_AU_3_1.remediation: Extend logging to include contextual data such as source IPs and outcome status. Use advanced logging features in cloud platforms to capture enriched audit information.

fedramp_moderate_AU_4.description: This control addresses ensuring sufficient storage capacity for audit logs to prevent data loss.
fedramp_moderate_AU_4.remediation: Monitor log storage usage and allocate scalable resources like cloud storage. Implement retention policies and archiving strategies to manage capacity effectively.

fedramp_moderate_AU_5.description: This control requires establishing procedures to respond to failures in audit logging processes, ensuring that audit records are not lost or compromised.
fedramp_moderate_AU_5.remediation: Develop and document incident response procedures for audit logging failures, including notification and recovery steps. Regularly test these procedures and assign roles for monitoring and response.

fedramp_moderate_AU_5_1.description: This control requires implementing warnings for storage capacity issues to prevent audit logging failures due to insufficient space.
fedramp_moderate_AU_5_1.remediation: Set up monitoring alerts for log storage capacity thresholds in cloud services like AWS CloudWatch or Azure Monitor. Configure automated notifications to administrators for proactive management.

fedramp_moderate_AU_5_2.description: This control requires real-time alerts for audit logging process failures to enable immediate response and mitigation.
fedramp_moderate_AU_5_2.remediation: Configure real-time alerting mechanisms using tools like AWS CloudTrail alarms or Azure Security Center. Ensure alerts are sent to designated personnel via email or messaging platforms.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6.description: This control requires regular review, analysis, and reporting of audit records to detect security incidents and ensure compliance.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6.remediation: Use automated tools like AWS CloudWatch Logs or Azure Log Analytics to set up log analysis and reporting. Implement scheduled reviews and generate compliance reports for stakeholders.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_1.description: This control requires integrating automated processes for audit record review and analysis to enhance efficiency and accuracy.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_1.remediation: Integrate audit logs with SIEM systems or cloud-native tools for automated analysis. Develop scripts or use IaC to automate log parsing and alert generation.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_3.description: This control requires correlating audit records from multiple repositories to provide a comprehensive security view.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_3.remediation: Use centralized logging solutions like AWS CloudTrail Lake or Azure Sentinel to aggregate and correlate logs. Implement data normalization and cross-repository analysis processes.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_4.description: This control requires central review and analysis of audit records to streamline security monitoring and reporting.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_4.remediation: Establish a centralized logging platform and assign a team for regular review. Use dashboards and automated reports to facilitate analysis and decision-making.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_5.description: This control requires integrated analysis of audit records to identify patterns and anomalies across systems.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_5.remediation: Deploy integrated analysis tools that combine logs from various sources. Train staff on using these tools and set up automated anomaly detection algorithms.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_6.description: This control requires correlating audit records with physical monitoring data to enhance overall security oversight.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_6.remediation: Integrate logical audit logs with physical access logs using unified security platforms. Develop procedures for cross-referencing data and investigating discrepancies.

fedramp_moderate_AU_6_7.description: This control requires defining permitted actions based on audit record analysis to enforce security policies.
fedramp_moderate_AU_6_7.remediation: Establish policies that link audit findings to allowed or restricted actions. Use RBAC and automated enforcement tools to implement these policies in cloud environments.

fedramp_moderate_AU_7.description: This control requires reducing audit records and generating reports to manage data volume and focus on relevant security events.
fedramp_moderate_AU_7.remediation: Implement log filtering and aggregation techniques to reduce noise. Use reporting tools to generate summaries and highlight critical events for review.

fedramp_moderate_AU_7_1.description: This control requires automatic processing for audit record reduction and report generation to improve efficiency.
fedramp_moderate_AU_7_1.remediation: Automate log reduction and reporting using cloud-native services or third-party tools. Schedule regular automated reports and adjust filters based on security needs.

fedramp_moderate_AU_8.description: This control requires using time stamps in audit records to ensure accurate event sequencing and forensic analysis.
fedramp_moderate_AU_8.remediation: Enable time stamping in all logging services and synchronize clocks using NTP. Use IaC to configure time stamp settings consistently across cloud resources.

fedramp_moderate_AU_9.description: This control requires protecting audit information from unauthorized access, modification, or deletion.
fedramp_moderate_AU_9.remediation: Implement encryption for audit logs at rest and in transit using cloud KMS. Apply strict RBAC policies to limit access to authorized personnel only.

fedramp_moderate_AU_9_2.description: This control requires storing audit information on separate physical systems or components to enhance security and availability.
fedramp_moderate_AU_9_2.remediation: Use isolated storage solutions like AWS S3 with cross-region replication or Azure Blob Storage in separate accounts. Ensure logical separation and access controls are enforced.

fedramp_moderate_AU_9_3.description: This control requires cryptographic protection of audit information to ensure integrity and confidentiality.
fedramp_moderate_AU_9_3.remediation: Enable encryption for audit logs using managed keys in cloud services. Implement digital signatures or hashing to detect tampering and ensure data integrity.

fedramp_moderate_AU_9_4.description: This control requires limiting access to audit information to a subset of privileged users to prevent unauthorized changes.
fedramp_moderate_AU_9_4.remediation: Define and enforce least-privilege access policies for audit logs using IAM roles. Regularly review and audit access permissions to ensure compliance.

fedramp_moderate_CA_1.description: This control requires establishing policies and procedures for security assessment and authorization processes.
fedramp_moderate_CA_1.remediation: Develop comprehensive security assessment policies and document procedures for implementation. Regularly update these documents and train staff on compliance requirements.

fedramp_moderate_CA_2.description: This control requires conducting control assessments to evaluate the effectiveness of security measures.
fedramp_moderate_CA_2.remediation: Use automated assessment tools like AWS GuardDuty or Azure Security Center to scan for vulnerabilities. Schedule regular assessments and document findings for remediation tracking.

fedramp_moderate_CA_2_1.description: This control requires using independent assessors for control assessments to ensure objectivity and accuracy.
fedramp_moderate_CA_2_1.remediation: Engage third-party auditors or use internal teams with separation of duties. Implement automated tools to support independent verification and reduce bias.

fedramp_moderate_CA_2_2.description: This control requires specialized assessments for specific controls or environments to address unique security risks.
fedramp_moderate_CA_2_2.remediation: Conduct targeted assessments using specialized tools or expertise, such as penetration testing for cloud APIs. Document specialized procedures and integrate findings into overall security plans.

fedramp_moderate_CA_2_3.description: This control requires leveraging assessment results from external organizations to enhance security posture.
fedramp_moderate_CA_2_3.remediation: Incorporate findings from external audits or shared threat intelligence into internal assessments. Use platforms that support data sharing and align with industry standards.

fedramp_moderate_CA_3.description: This control requires establishing agreements and procedures for secure information exchange with external entities.
fedramp_moderate_CA_3.remediation: Develop and maintain information exchange agreements, such as SLAs or MOUs. Implement encryption and access controls for data transfers and regularly review compliance.

fedramp_moderate_CA_3_6.description: This control requires transfer authorizations for information exchange to ensure proper oversight and security.
fedramp_moderate_CA_3_6.remediation: Establish authorization processes for data transfers, including approval workflows and documentation. Use automated tools to track and audit transfer activities.

fedramp_moderate_CA_5.description: This control requires developing and maintaining a plan of action and milestones (POA&M) to address security deficiencies.
fedramp_moderate_CA_5.remediation: Create a POA&M document to track identified weaknesses, assigned responsibilities, and remediation timelines. Regularly update and review the POA&M with stakeholders to ensure progress.

fedramp_moderate_CA_6.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining a formal authorization process for information systems, including risk assessments and approval by designated officials.
fedramp_moderate_CA_6.remediation: Develop and document an authorization process that includes risk assessments and approval workflows. Ensure designated officials review and approve system authorizations periodically, and maintain records of all authorizations.

fedramp_moderate_CA_7.description: This control requires continuous monitoring of security controls and system configurations to detect and respond to threats in real-time.
fedramp_moderate_CA_7.remediation: Implement automated monitoring tools like AWS CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, or GCP Monitoring to set up alerts for changes to network ACLs. Use IaC to enforce monitoring policies and ensure logs are reviewed regularly.

fedramp_moderate_CA_7_1.description: This control requires independent assessments of continuous monitoring activities to validate effectiveness and compliance.
fedramp_moderate_CA_7_1.remediation: Schedule regular independent audits using automated tools to assess monitoring configurations. Ensure separation of duties and use RBAC to restrict access to monitoring data, with findings documented and addressed.

fedramp_moderate_CA_7_4.description: This control requires monitoring for changes in risk levels and adjusting security measures accordingly based on continuous assessment.
fedramp_moderate_CA_7_4.remediation: Deploy risk monitoring tools that integrate with cloud services to track changes and anomalies. Use automated alerts and policy enforcement in IaC to adjust configurations dynamically based on risk assessments.

fedramp_moderate_CA_8.description: This control requires conducting penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities and assess the effectiveness of security controls.
fedramp_moderate_CA_8.remediation: Establish a penetration testing program with defined scope and frequency. Use authorized tools and methodologies, document findings, and remediate vulnerabilities through patch management and configuration updates.

fedramp_moderate_CA_8_1.description: This control requires penetration testing to be performed by independent agents or teams to ensure objectivity and thoroughness.
fedramp_moderate_CA_8_1.remediation: Engage third-party penetration testers with relevant certifications. Define clear testing protocols and scope in contracts, and ensure all findings are reviewed and addressed by internal security teams.

fedramp_moderate_CA_8_2.description: This control requires conducting red team exercises to simulate real-world attacks and test incident response capabilities.
fedramp_moderate_CA_8_2.remediation: Plan and execute red team exercises periodically, involving cross-functional teams. Document attack scenarios and response outcomes, and use lessons learned to improve security policies and procedures.

fedramp_moderate_CA_9.description: This control requires securing internal system connections through authentication and encryption to prevent unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_CA_9.remediation: Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher for load balancers and API gateways using IaC. Implement client certificate authentication and ensure all internal connections are encrypted and monitored for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_CM_1.description: This control requires developing and maintaining configuration management policies and procedures to govern system changes.
fedramp_moderate_CM_1.remediation: Create documented configuration management policies that outline roles, responsibilities, and procedures. Regularly review and update these policies to align with organizational changes and compliance requirements.

fedramp_moderate_CM_10.description: This control requires enforcing software usage restrictions to prevent unauthorized installation or use of software.
fedramp_moderate_CM_10.remediation: Implement software whitelisting policies and use endpoint management tools to restrict installations. Regularly audit software usage and update policies to reflect approved software lists.

fedramp_moderate_CM_11.description: This control requires managing user-installed software to ensure it does not compromise system security or compliance.
fedramp_moderate_CM_11.remediation: Establish policies that prohibit or restrict user-installed software. Use RBAC and automated tools to monitor and enforce these policies, with exceptions documented and approved by management.

fedramp_moderate_CM_12.description: This control requires identifying and documenting the location of information to support security and privacy management.
fedramp_moderate_CM_12.remediation: Use automated tools to inventory data locations across cloud environments. Implement strong password policies with minimum length requirements and document data flows in configuration management systems.

fedramp_moderate_CM_12_1.description: This control requires using automated tools to support the identification and tracking of information location for accuracy and efficiency.
fedramp_moderate_CM_12_1.remediation: Deploy CSPM or data discovery tools to automatically map and monitor data locations. Integrate these tools with IAM policies to enforce access controls and regularly update inventories.

fedramp_moderate_CM_14.description: This control requires using signed components to ensure the integrity and authenticity of software and firmware.
fedramp_moderate_CM_14.remediation: Implement policies that mandate digital signatures for all software components. Use automated validation tools during deployment and maintain a registry of signed components with periodic audits.

fedramp_moderate_CM_2.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining baseline configurations for information systems to ensure security and consistency.
fedramp_moderate_CM_2.remediation: Use configuration management tools like AWS SSM or Azure VM extensions to define and enforce baseline configurations. Implement IaC templates to automate deployment and ensure compliance across environments.

fedramp_moderate_CM_2_2.description: This control requires using automation to support the accuracy and currency of baseline configurations, reducing manual errors.
fedramp_moderate_CM_2_2.remediation: Automate configuration drift detection and remediation using tools like AWS Config or GCP OS Config. Schedule regular scans and updates through IaC pipelines to maintain configuration integrity.

fedramp_moderate_CM_2_3.description: This control requires retaining previous configurations to support rollback and audit trails in case of issues.
fedramp_moderate_CM_2_3.remediation: Implement version control for configuration files using tools like Git. Store historical configurations in secure repositories and ensure they are accessible for recovery and compliance audits.

fedramp_moderate_CM_2_7.description: This control requires configuring systems and components in high-risk areas with enhanced security measures to mitigate threats.
fedramp_moderate_CM_2_7.remediation: Identify high-risk areas through risk assessments and apply stricter configurations using IaC. Use encryption, network segmentation, and monitoring tools to protect these systems from potential attacks.

fedramp_moderate_CM_3.description: This control requires implementing configuration change control processes to manage and document system modifications.
fedramp_moderate_CM_3.remediation: Establish a change management workflow with approval steps and documentation. Use automated tools to enforce deletion protection and monitor changes, ensuring all modifications are tracked and audited.

fedramp_moderate_CM_3_1.description: This control requires automated documentation, notification, and prohibition of unauthorized configuration changes to maintain security.
fedramp_moderate_CM_3_1.remediation: Deploy automated change tracking systems that log and alert on configuration modifications. Use RBAC to restrict change permissions and integrate with ticketing systems for documentation and approval workflows.

fedramp_moderate_CM_3_2.description: This control requires testing, validation, and documentation of configuration changes to ensure they do not introduce vulnerabilities.
fedramp_moderate_CM_3_2.remediation: Implement a testing environment to validate changes before production deployment. Use automated testing tools and maintain detailed change logs to document validation results and approvals.

fedramp_moderate_CM_3_4.description: This control requires involving security and privacy representatives in configuration change processes to address compliance and risk.
fedramp_moderate_CM_3_4.remediation: Include security and privacy teams in change review boards. Use automated notifications to keep them informed of changes and ensure their input is documented in change management records.

fedramp_moderate_CM_3_6.description: This control requires managing cryptography within configuration changes to ensure encryption standards are maintained.
fedramp_moderate_CM_3_6.remediation: Enforce cryptographic policies through IaC, such as using TLS 1.2+ and key rotation schedules. Monitor cryptographic configurations with automated tools and audit them regularly for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_CM_4.description: This control requires conducting impact analyses of configuration changes to assess potential effects on system security and operations.
fedramp_moderate_CM_4.remediation: Develop a process for impact analysis that includes risk assessment and stakeholder review. Document analyses for all significant changes and update procedures based on findings to mitigate risks.

fedramp_moderate_CM_4_1.description: This control requires using separate test environments for impact analyses to prevent disruptions to production systems.
fedramp_moderate_CM_4_1.remediation: Maintain isolated test environments that mirror production configurations. Use IaC to provision these environments and ensure all changes are tested there before deployment, with clear documentation of test results.

fedramp_moderate_CM_4_2.description: This control requires performing impact analyses to verify that security controls remain effective after changes to the system. It involves assessing potential risks and ensuring controls are validated post-modification.
fedramp_moderate_CM_4_2.remediation: Establish a formal process for conducting impact analyses before implementing changes, documenting findings and verification steps. Use change management tools to track and review analyses, ensuring they are integrated into organizational procedures and reviewed by security teams.

fedramp_moderate_CM_5.description: This control mandates restricting access to system changes to authorized personnel only, preventing unauthorized modifications that could compromise security.
fedramp_moderate_CM_5.remediation: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) to limit change permissions to specific roles, such as administrators or change managers. Use identity and access management (IAM) policies to enforce least privilege, and maintain audit logs of access attempts for review.

fedramp_moderate_CM_5_1.description: This control requires automated enforcement of access restrictions for changes and generation of audit records to track access and modifications.
fedramp_moderate_CM_5_1.remediation: Deploy automated tools like AWS IAM, Azure Policy, or GCP Cloud IAM to enforce access controls and log all change activities. Configure alerts for unauthorized access attempts and regularly review audit logs to ensure compliance with change management policies.

fedramp_moderate_CM_5_5.description: This control involves limiting privileges for production and operational environments to reduce the risk of unauthorized changes or disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CM_5_5.remediation: Apply the principle of least privilege by restricting administrative access in production using IAM roles and policies. Implement separation of duties and use tools like AWS Organizations or Azure Management Groups to enforce privilege limitations across environments.

fedramp_moderate_CM_6.description: This control requires establishing and maintaining configuration settings for information systems to enforce security policies and reduce vulnerabilities.
fedramp_moderate_CM_6.remediation: Use infrastructure as code (IaC) tools like Terraform or CloudFormation to define and enforce secure configurations. Implement configuration management databases (CMDB) and automated compliance checks to monitor and remediate deviations from baseline settings.

fedramp_moderate_CM_6_1.description: This control mandates automated management, application, and verification of configuration settings to ensure consistency and security across systems.
fedramp_moderate_CM_6_1.remediation: Leverage automated configuration management tools such as Ansible, Chef, or Puppet to apply and verify settings. Integrate with CSPM platforms to continuously monitor for compliance and automatically remediate misconfigurations based on predefined policies.

fedramp_moderate_CM_6_2.description: This control requires responding to unauthorized changes in configuration settings to maintain system integrity and security.
