fedramp_moderate_CM_6_2.remediation: Set up real-time monitoring and alerting for configuration changes using tools like AWS Config, Azure Policy, or GCP Security Command Center. Establish incident response procedures to investigate and revert unauthorized changes promptly, and enforce change approval workflows.

fedramp_moderate_CM_7.description: This control enforces the principle of least functionality by restricting system components to only essential capabilities, reducing attack surfaces.
fedramp_moderate_CM_7.remediation: Configure network security groups and firewalls to block unnecessary ports and services. Use cloud-native tools like AWS Security Groups or Azure NSGs to apply deny-by-default rules, and regularly audit configurations to remove unused features or services.

fedramp_moderate_CM_7_1.description: This control requires periodic reviews of system functionality to ensure compliance with least functionality principles and identify unnecessary components.
fedramp_moderate_CM_7_1.remediation: Schedule regular audits using automated scanning tools to assess system configurations and services. Document findings and update policies to remove or restrict non-essential functions, and integrate reviews into change management processes.

fedramp_moderate_CM_7_2.description: This control involves preventing program execution on systems to limit functionality and reduce security risks from unauthorized software.
fedramp_moderate_CM_7_2.remediation: Implement application whitelisting or execution controls using tools like AWS Systems Manager or Azure AppLocker. Define policies to allow only approved programs and use endpoint protection platforms to block unauthorized executions in cloud environments.

fedramp_moderate_CM_7_5.description: This control mandates an allow-by-exception approach for authorized software, permitting only explicitly approved programs to run on systems.
fedramp_moderate_CM_7_5.remediation: Deploy software restriction policies via IaC or configuration management tools to create whitelists of allowed applications. Use cloud security tools to monitor and enforce these policies, and regularly update the whitelist based on business needs and security reviews.

fedramp_moderate_CM_8.description: This control requires maintaining an inventory of system components to track assets and manage security across the environment.
fedramp_moderate_CM_8.remediation: Use automated discovery tools like AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, or GCP Asset Inventory to create and update component inventories. Integrate with CMDB solutions and enforce tagging policies to ensure all assets are accounted for and monitored.

fedramp_moderate_CM_8_1.description: This control involves updating the system component inventory during installation and removal of components to maintain accuracy.
fedramp_moderate_CM_8_1.remediation: Automate inventory updates using cloud provider APIs and tools that trigger on resource creation or deletion. Implement IaC practices to ensure changes are logged and reflected in the inventory, and use event-driven workflows to synchronize data in real-time.

fedramp_moderate_CM_8_2.description: This control requires automated maintenance of the system component inventory to ensure it remains current and reliable.
fedramp_moderate_CM_8_2.remediation: Deploy scheduled scripts or cloud-native services like AWS Systems Manager Inventory or Azure Automation to regularly scan and update inventory records. Use APIs to pull data from multiple sources and validate entries against operational baselines.

fedramp_moderate_CM_8_3.description: This control mandates automated detection of unauthorized system components to identify and address potential security threats.
fedramp_moderate_CM_8_3.remediation: Set up continuous monitoring with CSPM tools to alert on unrecognized assets or deviations from the inventory. Use anomaly detection features and integrate with incident response systems to investigate and remediate unauthorized components promptly.

fedramp_moderate_CM_8_4.description: This control involves maintaining accountability information for system components, such as ownership and responsibility details.
fedramp_moderate_CM_8_4.remediation: Enforce tagging policies in cloud environments to include accountability fields like owner, department, and purpose. Use IAM roles and access logs to track component usage, and regularly audit tags to ensure they are complete and accurate for governance purposes.

fedramp_moderate_CM_9.description: This control requires developing and implementing a configuration management plan to guide secure configuration practices across the organization.
fedramp_moderate_CM_9.remediation: Document a configuration management plan outlining roles, procedures, and tools for managing system settings. Use version-controlled IaC templates and policy-as-code frameworks to enforce the plan, and conduct regular training and reviews to ensure adherence.

fedramp_moderate_CP_1.description: This control involves establishing policy and procedures for contingency planning to ensure organizational readiness for disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_1.remediation: Develop and document contingency policies that define roles, responsibilities, and processes for responding to incidents. Regularly review and update these procedures through tabletop exercises and integrate them with overall risk management frameworks.

fedramp_moderate_CP_10.description: This control requires capabilities for system recovery and reconstitution to restore operations after a disruption or failure.
fedramp_moderate_CP_10.remediation: Implement backup and disaster recovery solutions using cloud services like AWS Backup, Azure Site Recovery, or GCP Cloud Storage. Test recovery procedures regularly and ensure data encryption and access controls are maintained during restoration processes.

fedramp_moderate_CP_10_2.description: This control involves transaction recovery mechanisms to ensure data integrity and consistency during system restoration.
fedramp_moderate_CP_10_2.remediation: Use database features like transaction logs or cloud-native tools such as AWS RDS point-in-time recovery to enable transaction recovery. Implement automated scripts to verify data consistency post-recovery and conduct periodic drills to validate effectiveness.

fedramp_moderate_CP_10_4.description: This control mandates restoring system functionality within a specified time period to meet recovery objectives.
fedramp_moderate_CP_10_4.remediation: Define recovery time objectives (RTOs) and implement automated restoration workflows using IaC and orchestration tools. Monitor recovery times with logging and alerting, and optimize processes through regular testing to ensure they meet organizational requirements.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2.description: This control requires developing a contingency plan to address potential disruptions and ensure business continuity.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2.remediation: Create a contingency plan using cloud-native tools for backup, failover, and redundancy. Document procedures for activating the plan and use automation to deploy resources in alternate regions or zones during drills or actual incidents.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2_1.description: This control involves coordinating the contingency plan with related plans, such as incident response or disaster recovery, to ensure alignment.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2_1.remediation: Establish cross-functional teams to review and integrate contingency plans with other organizational procedures. Use shared documentation and communication channels to synchronize updates and conduct joint exercises to test coordination effectiveness.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2_2.description: This control requires capacity planning as part of contingency efforts to ensure sufficient resources are available during recovery.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2_2.remediation: Use cloud monitoring tools to assess resource usage and project needs for contingency scenarios. Implement auto-scaling and reserve instances in cloud environments to guarantee capacity, and regularly update plans based on performance data and business growth.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2_3.description: This control involves resuming mission and business functions as outlined in the contingency plan to minimize downtime and impact.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2_3.remediation: Define clear procedures for restoring critical functions using prioritized checklists and automated scripts. Test resumption processes in isolated environments and update plans based on lessons learned to improve recovery speed and reliability.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2_5.description: This control requires implementing deletion protection for critical cloud resources like load balancers and databases to prevent accidental deletion and ensure continuity of mission and business functions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2_5.remediation: Enable deletion protection on resources such as AWS ELBv2, Azure Load Balancer, GCP SQL instances, and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure load balancers using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) templates. Configure RBAC to restrict deletion permissions and enforce policies that mandate deletion protection for all production resources.

fedramp_moderate_CP_2_8.description: This control requires identifying and protecting critical assets by implementing deletion protection to prevent loss during contingency events.
fedramp_moderate_CP_2_8.remediation: Use automated checks to ensure deletion protection is enabled on critical assets across AWS, Azure, GCP, and Oracle Cloud. Apply IaC policies to tag and monitor these assets, and set up alerts for any configuration changes that remove protection.

fedramp_moderate_CP_3.description: This control requires providing contingency training to personnel to ensure they understand roles and procedures during disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_3.remediation: Develop and document a contingency training program with regular sessions and role-specific drills. Use governance tools to track attendance and completion, and update training materials based on lessons learned from tests and real incidents.

fedramp_moderate_CP_3_1.description: This control requires conducting contingency training through simulated events to test personnel readiness and response capabilities.
fedramp_moderate_CP_3_1.remediation: Schedule periodic simulated contingency events, such as tabletop exercises or live drills, and document outcomes. Review and refine procedures based on performance, and ensure all relevant staff participate to maintain preparedness.

fedramp_moderate_CP_4.description: This control requires testing the contingency plan to validate its effectiveness and ensure it can support recovery objectives.
fedramp_moderate_CP_4.remediation: Establish a testing schedule for the contingency plan, including full-scale exercises and partial tests. Document results, update the plan based on findings, and communicate changes to all stakeholders to ensure alignment.

fedramp_moderate_CP_4_1.description: This control requires coordinating contingency plan testing with related plans, such as disaster recovery or business continuity, to ensure consistency and avoid conflicts.
fedramp_moderate_CP_4_1.remediation: Align testing activities with other organizational plans by involving cross-functional teams and scheduling joint exercises. Review interdependencies and update coordination procedures regularly to maintain integrated response capabilities.

fedramp_moderate_CP_4_2.description: This control requires testing the contingency plan at an alternate processing site to verify it can support operations during a disruption.
fedramp_moderate_CP_4_2.remediation: Conduct tests at the alternate site to validate failover processes and recovery times. Document any issues, adjust configurations or procedures as needed, and ensure resources are pre-provisioned and accessible for rapid activation.

fedramp_moderate_CP_6.description: This control requires establishing an alternate storage site with backup capabilities to protect data and support recovery during contingencies.
fedramp_moderate_CP_6.remediation: Enable backup features on storage services like AWS EFS, Azure Backup, GCP Filestore, and Oracle File Storage using IaC. Configure backup policies with appropriate retention and encryption, and test restoration regularly to ensure data integrity.

fedramp_moderate_CP_6_1.description: This control requires ensuring the alternate storage site is geographically separated from the primary site to mitigate risks from localized disasters.
fedramp_moderate_CP_6_1.remediation: Deploy backups in different regions or availability zones using cloud provider tools. Use IaC to enforce separation policies and monitor compliance, ensuring data replication does not introduce single points of failure.

fedramp_moderate_CP_6_2.description: This control requires configuring the alternate storage site to meet defined Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO) for data restoration.
fedramp_moderate_CP_6_2.remediation: Set backup frequencies and retention periods in cloud services to align with RTO and RPO requirements. Automate monitoring and alerts for backup failures, and regularly test restoration to verify objectives are achievable.

fedramp_moderate_CP_6_3.description: This control requires ensuring the alternate storage site is accessible to authorized personnel during contingency events for data retrieval and recovery.
fedramp_moderate_CP_6_3.remediation: Configure access controls and network policies to allow secure connectivity to the alternate site. Use RBAC to limit permissions and test access under simulated outage conditions to confirm availability and performance.

fedramp_moderate_CP_7.description: This control requires establishing an alternate processing site to maintain operations if the primary site becomes unavailable.
fedramp_moderate_CP_7.remediation: Document and design the alternate site with redundant infrastructure and pre-provisioned resources. Regularly review and update the site configuration to match current operational needs and test failover procedures annually.

fedramp_moderate_CP_7_1.description: This control requires ensuring the alternate processing site is geographically separated from the primary site to reduce risk from common threats.
fedramp_moderate_CP_7_1.remediation: Select a site in a different region or cloud provider zone, and use IaC to deploy mirrored environments. Validate separation through network and dependency analysis, and update documentation to reflect any changes.

fedramp_moderate_CP_7_2.description: This control requires ensuring the alternate processing site is accessible to support operations during a contingency, with adequate bandwidth and connectivity.
fedramp_moderate_CP_7_2.remediation: Set up network routes and VPN connections to the alternate site, and test accessibility under load. Monitor performance metrics and adjust capacity as needed to maintain service levels during failover events.

fedramp_moderate_CP_7_3.description: This control requires establishing priority of service agreements for the alternate processing site to ensure critical functions receive resources during disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_7_3.remediation: Negotiate SLAs with cloud providers or internal teams to prioritize resource allocation. Document these agreements in contingency plans and test during drills to verify they meet operational requirements.

fedramp_moderate_CP_7_4.description: This control requires preparing the alternate processing site for use by configuring systems and ensuring they are ready to activate during a contingency.
fedramp_moderate_CP_7_4.remediation: Maintain the site with updated software, patches, and configurations through automated scripts. Conduct regular readiness checks and dry runs to validate that all components are operational and can be brought online quickly.

fedramp_moderate_CP_8.description: This control requires ensuring telecommunications services are available and resilient to support operations during contingency events.
fedramp_moderate_CP_8.remediation: Assess and document telecommunications dependencies, and establish redundant connections or backup providers. Test failover procedures periodically and update plans based on changes in service offerings or infrastructure.

fedramp_moderate_CP_8_1.description: This control requires implementing priority of service provisions for telecommunications to ensure critical communications are maintained during disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_8_1.remediation: Work with providers to secure priority service agreements and document them in contingency plans. Test these provisions during simulations to verify they function as expected and update as needed.

fedramp_moderate_CP_8_2.description: This control requires identifying and mitigating single points of failure in telecommunications infrastructure to enhance resilience.
fedramp_moderate_CP_8_2.remediation: Conduct risk assessments to pinpoint vulnerabilities and implement redundancies, such as diverse routing or multiple carriers. Regularly review and update configurations to eliminate single points of failure as technology evolves.

fedramp_moderate_CP_8_3.description: This control requires using separate primary and alternate telecommunications providers to reduce dependency on a single source.
fedramp_moderate_CP_8_3.remediation: Engage with multiple providers and configure failover mechanisms. Test the switch between providers during drills and monitor performance to ensure both meet required service levels.

fedramp_moderate_CP_8_4.description: This control requires reviewing and aligning with telecommunications providers' contingency plans to ensure compatibility and support during joint disruptions.
fedramp_moderate_CP_8_4.remediation: Request and evaluate provider contingency plans, and integrate them into organizational procedures. Coordinate with providers on testing and updates to maintain alignment and address any gaps.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9.description: This control requires implementing system backups with point-in-time recovery capabilities to protect data and support restoration during contingencies.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9.remediation: Enable point-in-time recovery on databases and storage services like AWS DynamoDB, Azure Cosmos DB, GCP Bigtable, and Oracle NoSQL using IaC. Configure backup policies with encryption and regular testing to ensure data availability and integrity.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9_1.description: This control requires testing backup reliability and integrity to ensure they can be restored successfully when needed.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9_1.remediation: Automate backup validation checks and conduct periodic restoration tests. Use monitoring tools to alert on failures and update backup procedures based on test results to maintain confidence in recovery capabilities.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9_2.description: This control requires testing restoration of backups using sampling methods to verify data consistency and completeness.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9_2.remediation: Implement a sampling strategy to restore random subsets of data regularly. Document outcomes and adjust backup configurations or processes if issues are found, ensuring all critical data is recoverable.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9_3.description: This control requires storing critical information backups separately from primary systems to protect against correlated failures.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9_3.remediation: Use isolated storage accounts or regions for backups, enforced through IaC policies. Apply encryption and access controls to secure these backups, and test separation by simulating primary site outages to confirm independence.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9_5.description: This control requires that system backups are transferred to an alternate storage site to ensure data availability and disaster recovery capabilities.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9_5.remediation: Configure automated backup policies in your cloud environment to replicate backups to a geographically separate storage location. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or CloudFormation to define backup and replication settings, ensuring compliance with data residency and recovery objectives.

fedramp_moderate_CP_9_8.description: This control mandates that system backups are cryptographically protected to prevent unauthorized access and ensure data integrity during storage and transfer.
fedramp_moderate_CP_9_8.remediation: Enable encryption for all backup data using cloud-native encryption services, such as AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, or Google Cloud KMS. Implement policies to enforce encryption at rest and in transit, and use IaC to automate encryption settings across backup resources.

fedramp_moderate_IA_1.description: This control involves establishing and maintaining policies and procedures for identification and authentication to guide organizational security practices.
fedramp_moderate_IA_1.remediation: Develop and document comprehensive identification and authentication policies, including roles, responsibilities, and review cycles. Regularly update these procedures to align with evolving threats and compliance requirements, and conduct training sessions to ensure staff awareness and adherence.

fedramp_moderate_IA_11.description: This control requires implementing re-authentication mechanisms for users after a defined period of inactivity or for sensitive actions to prevent unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_IA_11.remediation: Define and enforce re-authentication policies in identity management systems, such as setting session timeouts and requiring re-login for critical operations. Use cloud IAM tools to configure these settings and regularly audit logs to ensure compliance with organizational security standards.

fedramp_moderate_IA_12.description: This control involves verifying the identity of individuals before granting access to organizational systems, ensuring that users are who they claim to be.
fedramp_moderate_IA_12.remediation: Establish identity proofing procedures, such as document verification or multi-factor authentication, during user onboarding. Use identity providers with strong verification capabilities and maintain records of proofing activities to support audit and compliance efforts.

fedramp_moderate_IA_12_2.description: This control requires collecting and validating identity evidence, such as government-issued IDs or biometric data, to support identity proofing processes.
fedramp_moderate_IA_12_2.remediation: Implement processes to securely collect and store identity evidence, using encrypted channels and access controls. Regularly review and update evidence validation methods to align with regulatory standards and technological advancements.

fedramp_moderate_IA_12_3.description: This control mandates validating and verifying identity evidence to ensure its authenticity and accuracy before granting system access.
fedramp_moderate_IA_12_3.remediation: Use automated tools or manual checks to cross-reference identity evidence with trusted sources. Document validation steps and outcomes, and train personnel on fraud detection techniques to enhance the reliability of identity verification processes.

fedramp_moderate_IA_12_4.description: This control requires in-person validation and verification of identity for high-assurance scenarios, such as privileged access or sensitive operations.
fedramp_moderate_IA_12_4.remediation: Schedule in-person meetings or use video verification for critical identity checks, ensuring physical presence and document inspection. Maintain logs of in-person validations and integrate them into overall identity management workflows for audit purposes.

fedramp_moderate_IA_12_5.description: This control involves confirming the address of individuals as part of identity proofing to enhance verification accuracy and reduce fraud risk.
fedramp_moderate_IA_12_5.remediation: Verify addresses through official documents or third-party services, and update records periodically. Use address confirmation in combination with other proofing methods to strengthen identity assurance and comply with organizational policies.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2.description: This control requires implementing identification and authentication mechanisms for organizational users to ensure secure access to systems and data.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2.remediation: Enforce strong authentication methods, such as MFA, across all user accounts using cloud IAM policies. Use IaC to automate user provisioning and authentication settings, and regularly audit access logs to detect and remediate unauthorized activities.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_1.description: This control mandates multi-factor authentication for privileged accounts to add an extra layer of security and prevent unauthorized access to sensitive systems.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_1.remediation: Configure MFA for all privileged accounts in cloud IAM services, using hardware tokens or authenticator apps. Establish policies to require MFA for administrative actions and monitor compliance through regular access reviews and automated alerts.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_12.description: This control requires accepting PIV (Personal Identity Verification) credentials for authentication to align with federal standards and enhance security.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_12.remediation: Integrate PIV card readers or software with identity systems to support PIV-based authentication. Update authentication policies to prioritize PIV credentials and provide training for users on proper usage and handling of PIV devices.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_2.description: This control mandates multi-factor authentication for non-privileged accounts to protect against credential theft and unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_2.remediation: Enable MFA for all non-privileged user accounts using cloud-native MFA solutions. Use group policies in IAM to enforce MFA requirements and educate users on the importance of MFA for everyday security practices.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_5.description: This control involves combining individual authentication with group authentication mechanisms to balance security and usability in access management.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_5.remediation: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) that requires individual user authentication while assigning permissions based on group membership. Use IAM tools to manage group policies and ensure that individual credentials are always validated during access attempts.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_6.description: This control requires using separate devices for accessing accounts to reduce the risk of compromise from shared or compromised endpoints.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_6.remediation: Establish policies that mandate dedicated devices for sensitive account access, such as administrative workstations. Use device management solutions to enforce compliance and monitor for unauthorized device usage in cloud environments.

fedramp_moderate_IA_2_8.description: This control mandates implementing replay-resistant authentication mechanisms to prevent attackers from reusing captured credentials or tokens.
fedramp_moderate_IA_2_8.remediation: Use time-based one-time passwords (TOTP) or challenge-response protocols in authentication systems. Configure IAM settings to reject reused tokens and regularly update cryptographic keys to maintain replay resistance.

fedramp_moderate_IA_3.description: This control involves identifying and authenticating devices before granting network or system access to prevent unauthorized device connections.
fedramp_moderate_IA_3.remediation: Implement device certificates or MAC address filtering in network policies. Use cloud security groups and NACLs to restrict access to authenticated devices only, and regularly audit device inventories for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_IA_4.description: This control requires managing identifiers, such as user IDs and account names, to ensure they are uniquely assigned and properly controlled throughout their lifecycle.
fedramp_moderate_IA_4.remediation: Use IAM tools to automate identifier creation, modification, and deletion based on user roles and status. Implement least privilege principles and regularly review identifier assignments to prevent unauthorized access or orphaned accounts.

fedramp_moderate_IA_4_4.description: This control mandates identifying user status, such as active, inactive, or terminated, to manage access rights and reduce security risks from stale accounts.
fedramp_moderate_IA_4_4.remediation: Integrate HR systems with IAM to automatically update user status and trigger access changes. Use automated scripts or cloud-native features to disable or delete accounts based on status, and conduct periodic audits to ensure accuracy.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5.description: This control involves managing authenticators, such as passwords and tokens, to ensure they are strong, protected, and properly issued to users.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5.remediation: Enforce strong password policies, including minimum length and complexity, using cloud IAM settings. Use IaC to standardize authenticator management across environments and implement key rotation schedules for cryptographic authenticators.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_1.description: This control mandates implementing password-based authentication with requirements for strength, expiration, and protection to prevent unauthorized access.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_1.remediation: Configure password policies to enforce minimum length, complexity, and rotation intervals in IAM systems. Use MFA to complement passwords and monitor for weak or compromised passwords through regular security assessments.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_13.description: This control requires expiring cached authenticators after a defined period to reduce the window of opportunity for attackers to use stolen credentials.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_13.remediation: Set session timeout policies in authentication systems to clear cached credentials automatically. Use cloud security tools to enforce these settings and educate users on logging out of sessions to minimize caching risks.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_2.description: This control mandates implementing public key-based authentication, such as SSH keys, with proper management to secure access to systems and services.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_2.remediation: Use key management services to generate, rotate, and revoke public keys. Enforce key-based authentication policies in IAM and infrastructure configurations, and audit key usage regularly to detect unauthorized access.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_6.description: This control requires protecting authenticators from theft, loss, or compromise through physical and logical safeguards.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_6.remediation: Store authenticators in encrypted formats using hardware security modules or cloud KMS. Implement access controls to restrict who can view or modify authenticators, and use monitoring tools to detect and respond to unauthorized access attempts.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_7.description: This control mandates that no unencrypted static authenticators are embedded in applications, scripts, or configurations to prevent credential exposure.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_7.remediation: Use secrets management tools, such as AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault, to store and retrieve authenticators dynamically. Scan code and configurations for hardcoded credentials using automated tools, and enforce policies that require encryption for all static authenticators.

fedramp_moderate_IA_5_8.description: This control requires enforcing a minimum password length of at least 14 characters for system accounts to reduce the risk of brute-force attacks.
fedramp_moderate_IA_5_8.remediation: Configure password policies in cloud IAM services to set a minimum length of 14 characters. Use Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools like Terraform or CloudFormation to enforce this policy across all accounts and ensure regular audits for compliance.

fedramp_moderate_IA_6.description: This control requires that authentication feedback does not reveal sensitive information, such as whether a username exists, to prevent information disclosure during login attempts.
fedramp_moderate_IA_6.remediation: Implement generic error messages in authentication systems that do not differentiate between invalid usernames and passwords. Review and update application code and identity provider configurations to mask feedback details, and conduct regular security testing to verify compliance.

fedramp_moderate_IA_7.description: This control requires the use of cryptographic modules that are validated to FIPS 140-2 or equivalent standards for authentication processes to ensure secure cryptographic operations.
fedramp_moderate_IA_7.remediation: Deploy FIPS 140-2 validated cryptographic modules in cloud environments for authentication, such as using AWS KMS with FIPS endpoints or Azure Key Vault with FIPS compliance. Ensure that all authentication systems are configured to use these modules and document the implementation in security policies.

fedramp_moderate_IA_8.description: This control requires establishing identification and authentication mechanisms for non-organizational users, such as contractors or public users, to ensure secure access to systems.
fedramp_moderate_IA_8.remediation: Implement identity federation or external identity providers (e.g., OAuth, SAML) to manage authentication for non-organizational users. Use role-based access control (RBAC) to limit permissions and regularly review access logs to monitor for unauthorized activities.

fedramp_moderate_IA_8_1.description: This control requires accepting PIV (Personal Identity Verification) credentials from other federal agencies to enable secure cross-agency authentication.
fedramp_moderate_IA_8_1.remediation: Configure identity providers to accept PIV credentials via standards like PKI or SAML assertions. Ensure systems are integrated with federal identity management services and conduct interoperability testing to validate credential acceptance processes.

fedramp_moderate_IA_8_2.description: This control requires accepting external authenticators, such as those from third-party identity providers, to facilitate secure authentication for users outside the organization.
fedramp_moderate_IA_8_2.remediation: Integrate with external identity providers using protocols like OIDC or SAML, and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) for added security. Regularly audit these integrations to ensure they comply with organizational security policies and update configurations as needed.

fedramp_moderate_IA_8_4.description: This control requires using defined profiles or attributes for non-organizational users to standardize authentication and access management across systems.
fedramp_moderate_IA_8_4.remediation: Define and enforce user attribute profiles in identity management systems, such as using SCIM or custom claims in tokens. Implement automated provisioning and de-provisioning processes to maintain consistency and review profiles periodically for alignment with security requirements.

fedramp_moderate_IR_1.description: This control requires developing, documenting, and disseminating incident response policies and procedures to ensure a structured approach to handling security incidents.
fedramp_moderate_IR_1.remediation: Create and maintain incident response policies that outline roles, responsibilities, and procedures. Regularly update these documents based on lessons learned from drills or actual incidents, and ensure all relevant personnel are trained on the procedures.

fedramp_moderate_IR_2.description: This control requires providing incident response training to personnel to ensure they are prepared to effectively respond to security incidents.
fedramp_moderate_IR_2.remediation: Develop a training program that covers incident identification, reporting, and response steps. Conduct regular training sessions and simulations, and keep records of attendance and competency assessments to demonstrate compliance.

fedramp_moderate_IR_2_1.description: This control requires incorporating simulated events into incident response training to enhance preparedness and test response capabilities in a controlled environment.
fedramp_moderate_IR_2_1.remediation: Plan and execute simulated incident exercises, such as tabletop drills or red team activities, to test response procedures. Document outcomes and update training materials based on findings to improve future responses.

fedramp_moderate_IR_2_2.description: This control requires using automated training environments to provide realistic and scalable incident response training for personnel.
fedramp_moderate_IR_2_2.remediation: Deploy automated training platforms, such as cyber ranges or simulation tools, to create dynamic training scenarios. Regularly update these environments to reflect current threats and ensure they are accessible to all relevant staff members.

fedramp_moderate_IR_3.description: This control requires testing incident response capabilities to validate the effectiveness of policies and procedures in real-world scenarios.
fedramp_moderate_IR_3.remediation: Conduct periodic incident response tests, including full-scale exercises, to evaluate readiness. Analyze test results to identify gaps, update response plans accordingly, and ensure all team members are debriefed on lessons learned.

fedramp_moderate_IR_3_2.description: This control requires coordinating incident response testing with related plans, such as business continuity or disaster recovery, to ensure integrated and effective response efforts.
fedramp_moderate_IR_3_2.remediation: Align incident response testing schedules with other organizational plans and involve cross-functional teams in exercises. Document coordination efforts and update plans based on test outcomes to enhance overall resilience.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4.description: This control requires establishing and implementing incident handling procedures to detect, analyze, and respond to security incidents promptly.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4.remediation: Deploy automated security tools like AWS GuardDuty or Azure Security Center to monitor for high-severity findings. Configure alerts and response playbooks to automate containment actions, and regularly review incident logs to improve handling processes.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4_1.description: This control requires implementing automated incident handling processes to enable rapid detection and response to security incidents without manual intervention.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4_1.remediation: Use cloud-native security services with automation features, such as automated remediation rules in AWS GuardDuty or Azure Sentinel. Define clear escalation paths and integrate with ticketing systems to ensure incidents are tracked and resolved efficiently.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4_11.description: This control requires establishing an integrated incident response team that includes members from various departments to coordinate efforts during security incidents.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4_11.remediation: Form a cross-functional incident response team with defined roles and communication channels. Use collaboration tools and regular drills to ensure team readiness, and document response activities for continuous improvement.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4_2.description: This control requires implementing dynamic reconfiguration capabilities to adapt system defenses during incident response, such as isolating affected components.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4_2.remediation: Leverage cloud automation tools to dynamically adjust security groups, network ACLs, or resource configurations in response to incidents. Test these reconfiguration processes regularly to ensure they function as intended during actual events.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4_4.description: This control requires correlating information from multiple sources during incident handling to gain a comprehensive understanding of security events.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4_4.remediation: Integrate logs and alerts from various security tools into a SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) system for centralized analysis. Use correlation rules to identify patterns and automate responses, and regularly update these rules based on emerging threats.

fedramp_moderate_IR_4_6.description: This control requires addressing insider threats as part of incident handling procedures to detect and mitigate risks from authorized users.
fedramp_moderate_IR_4_6.remediation: Implement user behavior analytics (UBA) and monitoring tools to detect anomalous activities indicative of insider threats. Enforce least privilege access and conduct regular audits of user permissions to minimize risk.

fedramp_moderate_IR_5.description: This control requires monitoring security incidents to track their status and impact, ensuring timely and effective response efforts.
fedramp_moderate_IR_5.remediation: Establish a monitoring dashboard using tools like AWS CloudWatch or Azure Monitor to track incident metrics. Define key performance indicators (KPIs) for response times and regularly review monitoring data to identify areas for improvement.

fedramp_moderate_IR_5_1.description: This control requires using automated tracking, data collection, and analysis tools to enhance incident monitoring capabilities.
fedramp_moderate_IR_5_1.remediation: Deploy automated monitoring solutions that collect and analyze incident data in real-time. Configure these tools to generate reports and alerts, and ensure they are integrated with other security systems for comprehensive visibility.

fedramp_moderate_IR_6.description: This control requires establishing procedures for reporting security incidents to appropriate internal and external stakeholders.
fedramp_moderate_IR_6.remediation: Develop incident reporting templates and workflows that specify what information to report and to whom. Train personnel on reporting procedures and use communication tools to ensure timely notifications during incidents.

fedramp_moderate_IR_6_1.description: This control requires implementing automated reporting mechanisms to streamline the incident reporting process and ensure consistency.
fedramp_moderate_IR_6_1.remediation: Configure security tools to automatically generate and send incident reports to designated contacts. Test these automation workflows regularly to ensure they are reliable and update reporting criteria as needed based on regulatory requirements.

fedramp_moderate_IR_6_3.description: This control requires coordinating incident reporting with supply chain partners to address security incidents that may affect interconnected systems.
fedramp_moderate_IR_6_3.remediation: Establish communication protocols with supply chain partners for incident reporting and response. Include these protocols in contracts and conduct joint exercises to test coordination, updating agreements based on lessons learned.

fedramp_moderate_IR_7.description: This control requires providing incident response assistance to support affected parties during security incidents, such as offering technical guidance or resources.
fedramp_moderate_IR_7.remediation: Create a support plan that outlines how assistance will be provided during incidents, including contact points and available resources. Train response teams on assistance procedures and maintain documentation to ensure consistent support delivery.
